May 05, 2026 - Press release 1 of 2:

MYR GROUP INC. TO ATTEND BAIRD GLOBAL CONSUMER, TECHNOLOGY & SERVICES CONFERENCE IN JUNE

THORNTON, Colo., May 05, 2026 -- MYR Group Inc. (MYR Group) (NASDAQ: MYRG), a holding company of leading specialty contractors serving the electric utility infrastructure, commercial and industrial construction markets in the United States and Canada, announced it will attend the Baird Global Consumer, Technology & Services investor conference. MYR Groups Chief Executive Officer, Rick Swartz, Chief Financial Officer, Kelly Huntington, and Vice President, Investor Relations and Treasurer, Jennifer Harper, will meet with institutional investors during the Baird Global Consumer, Technology & Services Conference on June 4, 2026, in New York City. This event is only available to Baird clients.

About MYR Group Inc.MYR Group is a holding company of leading, specialty electrical contractors providing services throughout the United States and Canada through two business segments: Transmission & Distribution (T&D) and Commercial & Industrial (C&I). MYR Group subsidiaries have the experience and expertise to complete electrical installations of any type and size. Through their T&D segment they provide services on electric transmission, distribution networks, substation facilities, clean energy projects and electric vehicle charging infrastructure. Their comprehensive T&D services include design, engineering, procurement, construction, upgrade, maintenance and repair services. T&D customers include investor-owned utilities, cooperatives, private developers, government-funded utilities, independent power producers, independent transmission companies, industrial facility owners and other contractors. Through their C&I segment, they provide a broad range of services which include the design, installation, maintenance and repair of commercial and industrial wiring generally for data centers, clean energy projects, airports, hospitals, hotels, commercial and industrial facilities, manufacturing plants, processing facilities, water/waste-water treatment facilities, mining facilities, intelligent transportation systems, roadway lighting, signalization, stadiums and electric vehicle charging infrastructure. C&I customers include general contractors, commercial and industrial facility owners, government agencies and developers. For more information, visit myrgroup.com.

ContactJennifer Harper, Vice President, Investor Relations & Treasurer, MYR Group Inc., (847) 979-5835, investorinfo@myrgroup.com

Source: West Corporation

ACTIVITIES:

MYR Group, Inc., a Nasdaq-listed company, is a leading provider of electrical construction and maintenance services in the United States. The company operates through its subsidiaries, offering comprehensive solutions for the construction of transmission and distribution lines, substations, and commercial and industrial projects. Its core activities focus on supporting utility infrastructure, including high-voltage systems and renewable energy installations, which are essential for modernizing the power grid and facilitating clean energy transitions. In recent years, MYR Group's fastest-growing segments have been in renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar farms, driven by increasing regulatory mandates and investments in sustainable infrastructure. Additionally, the company benefits from growing demand in data center construction and electric vehicle charging networks, positioning it for continued expansion in the evolving energy sector. With a strong emphasis on safety and operational efficiency, MYR Group delivers reliable services to utility, commercial, and industrial clients, contributing to critical national infrastructure needs.

value.Title: DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - THE RECENT HEADLINES WITH PEER NEWS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-V373-RT40-C4D2-00000-00DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - THE RECENT HEADLINES WITH PEER NEWS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisSTOCKDAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - THE RECENT HEADLINES WITH PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP

PermID4296380340Websitehttp://www.davispolk.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress450 Lexington Avenue New York, NY 10017 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP, founded in 1849, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 1,000 lawyers across 10 offices, it serves clients in finance, technology, and healthcare, specializing in M&A, capital markets, and regulatory matters. In 2020, it advised on Morgan Stanleys $13 billion E*Trade acquisition and Gilead Sciences $21 billion Immunomedics deal. In 2024, it expanded its Menlo Park office with key hires like Paul Scrivano from Ropes & Gray. Generating over $1.5 billion annually, its culture emphasizes collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work. Competing with Cravath, Davis Polk stands out for its dealmaking prowess, ranking second in 2020 M&A league tables. Recent initiatives include AI-driven legal tech and ESG advisory services.

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIGENERGY'S HK IPO GAINS MOMENTUM WITH DAVIS POLK'S LEGAL EXPERTISE

Sigenergy Technology, a Chinese energy storage systems maker, has successfully navigated its initial public offering (IPO) in Hong Kong, raising HK$4.4 billion, equivalent to approximately $562 million. The transaction was facilitated by prominent law firms Davis Polk & Wardwell and Jia Yuan Law Offices, who provided critical legal advice throughout the process. This significant event was recorded on the 17th of April 2026.Founded in May 2022, Sigenergy specializes in distributed energy storage systems (DESS) and is headquartered in Shanghai. The company focuses on delivering innovative stackable, distributed solar-plus-storage all-in-one solutions, positioning itself strategically within the rapidly growing energy sector. Sigenergy has emerged as a leader in the market, commanding a 28.6 percent share in the global segment of stackable all-in-one distributed energy storage solutions, according to market data.The IPO was underwritten by a consortium of financial institutions, including CITIC Securities and BNP Paribas, with China International Capital Corporation (CICC) also playing a role as a joint sponsor. Legal support for the underwriters was provided by Linklaters and Jingtian & Gongcheng, with Linklaters’ team led by partner Christine Xu, who oversees Greater China ECM, and capital markets partner Lipton Li, alongside counsel Queenie Tong.In a related development, Mumbai-based Mindspace Business Parks REIT has made headlines with its acquisition of the International Tech Park in Chennai, valued at 30 billion rupees, or $321 million. This transaction was advised by Khaitan & Co, marking a significant investment in the Indian real estate sector. Furthermore, Indian asset manager SBI Funds Management is preparing for a substantial 130-billion-rupee IPO, with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas and Linklaters advising on the endeavor.The successful IPO of Sigenergy Technology not only highlights the company’s robust market position but also reflects the growing investor interest in renewable energy solutions. As the demand for energy storage systems continues to rise, Sigenergy is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the sector.

+ DAVIS POLK EXPANDS WESTWARD WITH SKADDEN PARTNER JASON RUSSELL

Davis Polk & Wardwell is set to expand its footprint by opening a new office in Los Angeles, recruiting Jason Russell, the head of Skadden Arps Slate Meagher & Flom’s LA office. This strategic move is expected to enhance Davis Polk's capabilities in a city known for its vibrant legal landscape, particularly in the sectors of sports and entertainment. The firm plans to officially open its Los Angeles office on 14th April 2026.Russell, a distinguished litigator with years of experience at Skadden, has represented prominent clients such as the National Basketball Association, Christie’s Inc., MGA Entertainment Inc., and Credit Acceptance Corp. His expertise in high-stakes litigation and complex class actions positions Davis Polk favorably in the competitive Los Angeles market. Additionally, Russell has served on Skadden’s policy committee, underscoring his influence within the firm.The opening of the Los Angeles office is a significant milestone for Davis Polk, which has been gaining prominence in the legal sector. The firm had a remarkable year in 2025, achieving a 26% increase in revenue to $3.2 billion, which was the highest growth among major law firms. In comparison, Skadden recently crossed the $4 billion revenue threshold but did not match Davis Polk's growth rate. This success has translated into attractive partner profits, with Davis Polk partners earning an average of $9.8 million.Davis Polk is not alone in recognizing the potential of the Los Angeles market. The city has seen a surge of interest from leading law firms, including Willkie Farr & Gallagher, which established an office in 2021 and has since expanded to over 100 attorneys. Moreover, Paul Weiss Rifkind Wharton & Garrison recently opened an office in LA following a strategic acquisition from Kirkland & Ellis, indicating strong competition for top legal talent and clients.As Davis Polk prepares for its Los Angeles debut, further hiring plans are already underway. With Jason Russell at the helm and an ambitious growth strategy, the firm aims to carve out a significant presence in the region. The legal industry will be closely watching how this expansion unfolds in a city that has long been a hub for major law firms.

+ DAVIS POLK'S LIGHTYEAR CAPITAL SECURES $2.5B FOR SIXTH FUND

Lightyear Capital, a private equity firm co-founded by Davis Polk & Wardwell, has successfully raised $2.5 billion for its sixth fund. This significant capital infusion highlights the firm's robust position in the asset management space and reflects investor confidence in its investment strategy and market outlook. The announcement was made public on April 14, 2026.The latest fund is poised to target investments in the financial services and technology sectors, which have shown resilience and growth potential in recent years. With this capital, Lightyear Capital aims to capitalize on emerging opportunities within these industries, leveraging its extensive network and expertise to identify lucrative investment avenues. Davis Polk's longstanding experience in providing legal services to financial institutions further strengthens Lightyear's operational capabilities.The fundraising comes amid a competitive landscape for private equity firms, where securing investor commitments has become increasingly challenging. Lightyear Capital's ability to attract significant backing can be attributed to its track record of strong returns and strategic investment decisions. The firm plans to focus on both established companies and innovative startups, providing them with the necessary support to scale their operations effectively.Davis Polk & Wardwell's involvement in Lightyear Capital is not merely advisory; the firm's attorneys have been integral in structuring the fund and navigating the regulatory landscape. Their expertise ensures that Lightyear operates within legal parameters while maximizing investor returns, a critical factor for stakeholders in today’s complex financial environment. The collaboration between the two entities underscores the synergy between legal prowess and investment acumen.Looking ahead, investors will be keen to see how Lightyear Capital deploys its newly raised capital and whether it can maintain its momentum in generating returns. As the market evolves, the firm’s strategies will be closely scrutinized, particularly in the context of economic fluctuations and sector-specific challenges. With Davis Polk's backing, Lightyear Capital is well-positioned to make a significant impact in the private equity arena.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES UNPRECEDENTED REVENUE GROWTH IN LONDON

Debevoise & Plimpton has reported a remarkable revenue surge of 40% in its London office, reaching a record $268 million. This significant increase has drawn attention in the legal industry and among investors seeking insights into the firm's growth trajectory. The results, announced on 7th April 2026, highlight the firm's robust performance in a competitive market.The leap in revenue is attributed to an uptick in demand for legal services across various sectors, including corporate law and dispute resolution. Debevoise & Plimpton's strategic focus on high-value client engagements and its ability to adapt to changing market conditions have played a key role in this growth. The firm has consistently positioned itself as a leader in providing comprehensive legal solutions to its clientele.In light of this impressive performance, analysts are keenly observing how Debevoise & Plimpton plans to sustain this momentum moving forward. The firm has expressed its commitment to investing in talent and expanding its service offerings, which may further bolster its market position. This proactive approach could potentially attract more clients and drive additional revenue growth in the coming years.The legal sector has witnessed a notable transformation in recent years, and firms like Debevoise & Plimpton that embrace innovation and adapt to new challenges may be well-positioned for continued success. As competition intensifies, maintaining a strong financial performance will be crucial for the firm to retain its competitive edge.As Debevoise & Plimpton celebrates this milestone, the legal community is left wondering whether this growth trend will continue. Investors and clients alike will be looking for signs of sustained performance, as the firm navigates the complexities of the ever-evolving legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES LANDMARK VICTORY FOR YPF S.A. IN $16 BILLION CASE

In a significant legal victory, Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has successfully secured an affirmance from the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit regarding its summary judgment win on behalf of YPF S.A., the largest energy producer in Argentina. This decision, made on 30th March 2026, effectively dismisses claims for over $16 billion in damages and interest, marking a pivotal moment in a case that has lingered in the Southern District of New York since 2015.The Second Circuit upheld the District Court’s finding that YPF did not have any contractual obligations to Petersen Energ a Inversora, S.A.U., Petersen Energ a, S.A.U., or Eton Park Capital Management, L.P., along with its associated funds. This ruling is seen as a major victory for YPF, affirming its non-liability for the claims advanced by the plaintiffs, who also included litigation funder Burford Capital.The case traces its roots back to Argentina's 2012 intervention and subsequent expropriation of 51% of YPF's capital stock. The litigation commenced in 2015, and Debevoise took over YPF's defense in December 2020 after earlier motions to dismiss were denied. Following two years of extensive discovery involving numerous expert testimonies, both the plaintiffs and YPF, alongside the Republic of Argentina, sought summary judgment in 2022.In 2023, the District Court granted YPF’s motion for summary judgment in its entirety, adopting Debevoise's arguments that YPF was not obliged under its bylaws to compel the Republic to conduct a tender offer or restrict the Republic's corporate governance rights over the expropriated shares. Conversely, the District Court largely granted the plaintiffs’ motion for summary judgment against the Republic, resulting in a judgment exceeding $16 billion against it in September 2023.Following appeals from all parties involved, the case was fully briefed and argued before the Second Circuit in October 2025. The affirmation of YPF's victory not only reinforces the firm's position but also highlights the complexities involved in international energy disputes and expropriation cases, setting a notable precedent in the legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE ELEVATES JEFFREY SULLIVAN KC AND INA POPOVA TO EUROPEAN DISPUTES LEADERSHIP

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has announced the appointment of partners Jeffrey Sullivan KC and Ina Popova as Co-Chairs of European Disputes, effective immediately. This strategic move enhances the firm's presence in key markets, particularly in Paris, Frankfurt, and Luxembourg. The announcement comes on the 17th of March, 2026, highlighting the firm's commitment to strengthening its capabilities in international dispute resolution across mainland Europe.Catherine Amirfar, who serves as Co-Chair of Debevoise's global International Dispute Resolution Group, expressed confidence in the leadership of Sullivan KC and Popova. She stated, 'Ina and Jeff’s leadership will build on Debevoise’s long-standing strength in major disputes across mainland Europe, ensuring we continue to deliver the highest level of advocacy and strategic counsel to our clients.' Their appointments align with the firm's strategy of appointing regional leaders to drive its dispute resolution practice worldwide, joining other prominent leaders in Latin America and Asia.Jeffrey Sullivan KC is already recognized as a leading figure in international arbitration and public international law. In his new role, he will continue to focus on high-stakes commercial and investor-state arbitrations, representing a diverse clientele that includes companies and private equity sponsors. H. Rodgin “Rodge” Cohen, senior chair at Sullivan & Cromwell, has been a transformative force in the banking industry for over five decades. On 19th February 2026, during an event hosted by NYU Law's Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance (ICGF), Cohen shared his experiences and insights on the evolution of the modern financial system. Clayton, who has previously worked alongside Cohen at Sullivan & Cromwell, highlighted the profound changes in banking regulation since the 1970s. Cohen emphasized the significance of the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act, noting that these measures have drastically changed access to banking services, reducing the percentage of unbanked Americans from one-third to just five percent.A defining moment in Cohen's career came during the 2008 global financial crisis, where he represented several major banks in efforts to stabilize the financial system. Reflecting on the crisis, Cohen recalled the critical hours leading up to the government’s decision to intervene after the collapse of Lehman Brothers. He stated, 'We were on the edge of a cliff,' emphasizing the urgency and gravity of the situation. His efforts during this tumultuous time solidified his reputation as a leading financial services lawyer.Cohen's influence extends beyond crisis management; he has also played a crucial role in regulatory changes that have transformed the banking industry. For instance, his advocacy for interstate banking in the 1980s allowed banks to expand their operations across state lines, significantly reshaping the banking landscape. Clayton praised Cohen’s ability to interpret statutes creatively, which has been vital for navigating the complexities of financial regulation.As the conversation turned to future challenges, Cohen highlighted the importance of effective cryptocurrency regulation. He warned of potential pitfalls if regulations do not keep pace with the evolving market, likening the situation to Gresham’s Law. 'Bad regulation drives out good regulation,' he cautioned, stressing the need for robust frameworks to manage the risks associated with cryptocurrency. Cohen's insights serve as a reminder of the ongoing evolution of the financial sector and the critical role of firms like Sullivan & Cromwell in shaping its future.

The collaboration with Simpson Thacher exemplifies the synergy between legal expertise and investment strategy, a combination that is becoming increasingly crucial in today’s fast-paced financial environment.

+ SIMPSON THACHER BOLSTERS CAPITAL STRUCTURE SOLUTIONS WITH NEW PARTNER ADDITIONS

Simpson Thacher & Bartlett announced the addition of partners Christine Bae and Jacob Ruby to its Capital Structure Solutions Practice, reinforcing the firm's commitment to complex financing and restructuring matters. This move comes as part of the firm's strategy to enhance its capabilities in response to growing demand for sophisticated capital structure solutions, following the establishment of the practice in early 2026.The date of the announcement, 18th March 2026, marks a significant step for Simpson Thacher as it aims to provide comprehensive advisory services in areas such as liability management, private credit, and special situations. The practice is led by David Nemecek, who has been pivotal in shaping the firm's response to the evolving landscape of capital structure complexities.Bae and Ruby bring a wealth of experience to Simpson Thacher, having previously advised a diverse clientele that includes sponsors, companies, lenders, and investors on intricate capital structure challenges. Their expertise spans liability management exercises and distressed transactions, focusing on crafting bespoke solutions to tackle strategic and financial hurdles faced by clients.The Capital Structure Solutions Practice integrates the firm’s existing teams in liability management, special situations, and restructuring with its broader financing capabilities. This integration aims to offer coordinated guidance across various aspects of financing strategy, credit solutions, distressed M&A, and Chapter 11 restructurings, aligning with Simpson Thacher’s commitment to delivering client-centric advice.David Nemecek emphasized the importance of these hires, stating, "Christine and Jacob bring deep technical capability, sound judgment, and the ability to craft bespoke solutions for clients navigating their most complex capital structure challenges." Both Bae and Ruby expressed enthusiasm for joining Simpson Thacher, highlighting the firm's collaborative culture and its integrated platform as key factors that will enhance their practice.

+ WACHTELL, LIPTON, ROSEN & KATZ LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SYSCO'S $29 BILLION BID: JONES DAY AND WACHTELL AT THE HELM

Sysco Corporation has engaged Jones Day for antitrust counsel as it moves to acquire rival Jetro Restaurant Depot for a staggering $29 billion. The legal representation from Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz LLP marks a significant step in this high-stakes transaction, which was confirmed on 31st March 2026.The acquisition aims to bolster Sysco’s position in the competitive food distribution market, which has been facing increasing pressure from rising costs and supply chain challenges. With Jetro's established network in the restaurant supply sector, Sysco is looking to enhance its operational capabilities and expand its customer base. Legal experts anticipate a thorough review process, given the size of the deal and its implications for market competition.Jones Day, known for its robust antitrust practice, will likely guide Sysco through regulatory scrutiny as the deal is assessed by competition authorities. Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz, renowned for its expertise in complex mergers and acquisitions, will also play a critical role in navigating the legal landscape surrounding this transaction. Both firms are expected to leverage their significant experience in similar high-profile deals to ensure a smooth process.As the largest food distributor in North America, Sysco's aggressive expansion strategy through this acquisition signifies a broader trend in the food service industry, where companies are actively seeking consolidation to improve efficiency and customer service. The outcome of this deal could reshape the competitive dynamics within the sector and potentially set a precedent for future mergers.Investors will be closely monitoring the progress of this acquisition, as it could have far-reaching implications not only for Sysco and Jetro but also for the entire food distribution market. The collaboration between Jones Day and Wachtell highlights the critical importance of experienced legal guidance in navigating complex mergers, particularly in an era of heightened regulatory scrutiny.

+ WACHTELL LIPTON GUIDES PUBLIC STORAGE IN $10.5BN ACQUISITION

Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz LLP and Clifford Chance are taking lead roles in a monumental $10.5 billion transaction aimed at establishing a self-storage powerhouse. The deal was officially announced on 24th March 2026, with Wachtell Lipton advising its long-standing client, Public Storage, the largest self-storage company in the United States by market capitalization, on its acquisition of National Storage Affiliates.Clifford Chance is representing National Storage Affiliates in this significant all-stock transaction, which will boost Public Storage's market capitalization to approximately $57 billion. The acquisition will enable the combined entity to oversee an impressive square footage equivalent to that of a small city, expanding its footprint significantly in key U.S. markets.Leading Wachtell Lipton's team is Adam Emmerich, co-chair of the corporate department, alongside M&A partner Kyle Diamond. Emmerich has a history of advising Public Storage on major transactions, including its $6 billion acquisition of Shurgard in 2006 and the $2.2 billion purchase of Simply Self Storage from Blackstone Real Estate Income Trust in 2023. The firm’s expertise is expected to play a crucial role in navigating the complexities of this latest acquisition.Clifford Chance's team is spearheaded by M&A partners Chang-Do Gong and Robert Chung, who have both recently joined the firm from White & Case. They are joined by capital markets partner Andrew Epstein and real estate partner Jeanne Roig-Irwin. Meanwhile, DLA Piper is providing real estate financing counsel to Public Storage, with its team led by partner Amy Carbins and supported by New York-based partner Viktor Sapezhnikov.Under the terms of the agreement, shareholders of National Storage will receive 0.14 of a share of Public Storage common stock for each National Storage share they own, translating to a total consideration of $41.68 per share. The transaction is anticipated to close in the third quarter of 2026, following the formation of a joint venture that will encompass 313 properties with an estimated value of approximately $3.3 billion.

+ ELON MUSK'S X CORP WITHDRAWS $90 MILLION LAWSUIT AGAINST WACHTELL LIPTON

In a surprising turn of events, Elon Musk's X Corp, formerly known as Twitter, has decided to drop its contentious $90 million lawsuit against the prominent law firm Wachtell Lipton Rosen & Katz. This decision comes just one day after the legal drama escalated, marking the end of a turbulent chapter in the saga surrounding Musk's $44 billion takeover of the social media platform. The lawsuit had alleged that Wachtell Lipton improperly paid bonuses during the acquisition process, a claim that has now been dismissed with prejudice.The dismissal of the lawsuit leaves a significant impact on discussions surrounding corporate governance and legal fee practices among law firms and their clients. As X Corp moves forward, the implications of this legal withdrawal may resonate beyond its own operations, potentially influencing how other corporations approach legal disputes and compensation structures. The legal landscape for high-stakes mergers and acquisitions continues to evolve, and this case serves as a noteworthy reference point.Wachtell Lipton, known for its expertise in corporate law, had previously defended itself vigorously against the allegations made by X Corp. The law firm's response highlighted the complex nature of mergers and acquisitions and the role of legal counsel in navigating these intricate processes. By dropping the lawsuit, X Corp appears to be seeking resolution and stability after a period of uncertainty that followed Musk's acquisition of Twitter in late .Industry observers will be keen to analyze the broader repercussions of this development. The legal community, along with corporate executives, may take note of the potential challenges and risks associated with legal claims of this magnitude. As X Corp transitions from this chapter, its focus will likely shift toward operational strategies and enhancing the platform's performance in a competitive social media landscape.This conclusion to the legal dispute underscores the unpredictability of corporate litigation, particularly in high-profile cases involving major figures like Elon Musk. As X Corp navigates its next steps, stakeholders will be closely monitoring how the firm adapts and evolves in response to both market conditions and legal frameworks.

+ CRAVATH SWAINE & MOORE PARTNER VENTURES INTO CORPORATE INVESTIGATIONS

In a notable shift within the legal landscape, a partner from Cravath Swaine & Moore has announced the formation of a new boutique law firm dedicated to corporate investigations. This move comes as businesses increasingly seek specialized counsel to navigate complex regulatory environments and compliance challenges. The partner, whose experience at Cravath has positioned them as a leader in the field, aims to leverage their expertise to provide tailored services to clients facing intricate corporate scrutiny.On 9th March 2026, the launch of this boutique firm signals a growing trend among established legal practitioners to break away from traditional law firm structures in favor of more agile, focused practices. The new firm is expected to offer services ranging from internal investigations, compliance assessments, to risk management strategies. This initiative not only reflects a shift in client needs but also highlights the evolving nature of legal services in the corporate sector.Cravath Swaine & Moore has long been recognized for its prowess in corporate law, and the establishment of this boutique by one of its partners further emphasizes the firm’s influential role in the legal community. As regulatory environments become increasingly complex, the demand for specialized knowledge in corporate investigations is likely to grow. The new firm is set to cater to corporations facing challenges related to compliance violations and internal misconduct, providing a fresh perspective backed by years of experience in high-stakes legal matters.Industry experts predict that this boutique firm will attract a diverse clientele, including Fortune 500 companies, private equity firms, and startups. The partner’s extensive network and reputation built during their tenure at Cravath Swaine & Moore will be instrumental in establishing the new firm's credibility and market presence. Clients are expected to benefit from a personalized approach that larger firms might struggle to provide, particularly in sensitive investigations where discretion and expertise are paramount.As the legal landscape continues to evolve, the emergence of such specialized firms underscores the importance of adaptability within the legal profession. The launch of this corporate investigations boutique represents not only a strategic career move for the Cravath partner but also a response to the pressing needs of corporate clients. The legal community will be watching closely to see how this venture develops and whether it will set a precedent for similar initiatives in the future.

+ BIOCON'S $1.2 BILLION BIOSIMILARS DEAL INVOLVED MULTIPLE LEGAL ADVISORS

Biocon, an Indian biopharmaceutical company, has finalized a significant $1.2 billion deal aimed at fully consolidating its biosimilars business by acquiring the remaining stakes from minority partners. This strategic move is expected to streamline Biocon's operations and enhance its market position in the growing biosimilars sector. On the 11th of December 2025, the company disclosed this acquisition, marking a pivotal shift in its growth trajectory.The deal sees Biocon acquiring stakes from various minority partners, including a notable transaction with Serum Institute Life Sciences, which was advised by Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas. Viatris, having sold its remaining stake for $815 million, received guidance from Khaitan & Co and Cravath, Swaine & Moore. The acquisition also involved other sellers, including True North and Tata Capital Growth Fund II, which was advised by Veritas Legal, further highlighting the collaborative nature of this transaction.Shardul Amarchand Mangaldas (SAM) led the advisory team for Biocon, with partners Raghubir Menon and Natashaa Shroff at the forefront. The deal team also included principal associate Tarumoy Chaudhuri and senior associate Kriti Saraswat, alongside a comprehensive support team. This consolidation comes as Biocon has been eyeing the completion of its integration with Viatris and the refinancing of its debt, as reported by Reuters.The legal landscape for this transaction was extensive, with multiple law firms providing specialized guidance. Khaitan & Co's transaction team, led by partners Rabindra Jhunjhunwala and Abhishek Dadoo, played a crucial role in advising on various aspects of the deal. Meanwhile, Cravath, Swaine & Moore's team, headed by partners Mark I Greene and Aaron M Gruber, focused on financing matters, showcasing the depth of expertise involved in this high-stakes transaction.As Biocon moves forward with its plans, the successful consolidation of its biosimilars business is anticipated to strengthen its competitive edge in the biopharmaceutical market. The company's strategic maneuvers, supported by a robust legal framework, position it well for future growth in an increasingly competitive sector.

+ CRAVATH SWAINE & MOORE UNVEILS YEAR-END BONUSES, ASSOCIATES TO EARN UP TO $140K

Cravath Swaine & Moore, one of the largest U.S. law firms, has officially launched the year-end bonus season by announcing that its associates will receive payouts of up to $140,000. On 20th November 2025, the New York-based firm communicated to its associates via an internal memo that annual bonuses will range from $15,000 for first-year associates to $115,000 for the most senior associates, according to information reported by Above the Law.In addition to the standard bonuses, Cravath plans to offer special bonuses ranging from $6,000 to $25,000, mirroring the payouts recently made by rival firm Milbank. This strategic move reinforces Cravath’s position as a traditional first mover in the competitive landscape of associate compensation within the legal sector. While the firm declined to provide further comments, the bonus structure aligns with expectations set by previous years.Following Cravath's announcement, other major firms are already responding. Paul Hastings, for instance, quickly disclosed its own year-end and special bonuses, which reflect a similar scale to those set by Cravath. A spokesperson from Paul Hastings also chose not to comment, indicating that the firm is likely following the trend established by Cravath.The bonuses announced by Cravath Swaine & Moore are consistent with those distributed by large U.S. law firms last year, as many associates now earn annual salaries ranging from $225,000 to $435,000 depending on their class year. The competitive nature of the legal profession often leads firms to match salary and bonus figures to retain top talent, and Cravath’s decision is expected to influence other firms in the sector.Looking ahead, U.S. law firms are poised for a profitable 2025, with large and midsized firms experiencing a significant increase in client demand, particularly in transactional practices. This positive outlook was highlighted in a recent analysis of firm financial data, suggesting that the legal industry is gearing up for another successful year, further emphasizing the importance of competitive compensation packages like those offered by Cravath.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ MILBANK'S STRATEGIC PLAN TO COMBAT INVASIVE BEETLE THREATENS ASH TREES

MILBANK — In a proactive response to an invasive species threat, city leaders in Milbank are implementing a comprehensive strategy to protect their ash trees from the emerald ash borer. The infestation, first detected last summer, poses a significant risk to the northeastern South Dakota city’s tree canopy, which is composed of over 50% ash trees, including those found in local parks and the cemetery. On 21st April 2026, city administrator Steve Pendergrass outlined the financial implications of the crisis, indicating that the cost of removing the trees could reach up to $4 million.Conversely, Milbank's strategy to develop what it describes as 'herd immunity' against the beetle will require an estimated $2.4 million over the next 12 years. This approach aims to enhance the resilience of the ash trees through targeted treatment and monitoring, allowing the city to preserve its green spaces while combating the invasive pest. The strategy is seen as a viable model that could be adapted by other cities in South Dakota facing similar threats to their tree populations.The emerald ash borer, a woodboring beetle, has wreaked havoc across various regions of the United States, leading to widespread tree loss. Milbank's initiative to promote herd immunity reflects an understanding of the ecological importance of maintaining a robust tree canopy, which provides numerous environmental benefits including improved air quality, water retention, and habitat for wildlife. By investing in the health of these trees, Milbank hopes to create a sustainable urban forest that can withstand the challenges posed by invasive species.As Milbank embarks on this ambitious project, the collaboration between local officials, environmental experts, and community members will be crucial. The city plans to implement a series of treatments on a phased basis, focusing on the most vulnerable areas first. This careful approach not only aims to save the existing trees but also hopes to foster a greater awareness of the importance of tree health and biodiversity in urban environments.The successful execution of Milbank's strategy could serve as a template for other communities grappling with similar challenges. As the city takes steps to protect its ash trees, it stands as a testament to the importance of proactive environmental stewardship, offering a beacon of hope for cities that wish to maintain their natural heritage in the face of invasive threats.

+ MILBANK ANALYZES CFTC'S NEW GUIDANCE ON PREDICTION MARKETS

In a recent webinar, Milbank attorneys examined the Commodity Futures Trading Commission’s (CFTC) latest guidance on prediction markets, which was released last week. The event, titled 'The CFTC’s New Guidance on Prediction Markets: What You Need to Know,' took place on Tuesday. This follows the CFTC's announcement on 17th March 2026, introducing a regulatory expectations advisory and an Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking, initiating a 45-day public comment period that concludes on 30th April.Milbank Partner Josh Sterling opened the discussion by highlighting the remarkable growth of prediction markets, which surged from $3 billion in trading volume in January 2025 to $27 billion in January 2026. This growth has attracted attention not only from those aiming to rejuvenate financial markets but also from the courts and state regulators. The session featured Milbank litigation partners Grant Mainland, Matt Laroche, and John Williams, who provided insights on the current state of the prediction market industry and the implications of the CFTC's recent announcements for market operators.Laroche emphasized that the CFTC's advisory indicates the commission's belief that contracts related to sports events fall under its jurisdiction, which he described as a significant clarification. He stated, 'The purpose of the advisory was to provide right up from that the CFTC is recognizing it is a unique financial asset class that is rapidly increasing and provides a reliable source of information for a variety of uses.' This recognition could influence ongoing litigation regarding the classification of such contracts.Williams noted that the notice of rulemaking represents a strong stance by the CFTC to assert jurisdiction over these contracts, though it does not clearly outline future regulatory directions. He remarked on the extensive list of questions posed for public comment, which invites input on how event contracts might contribute to hedging, price risk, and information dissemination. 'It’s a good sign the commission is thinking about those things,' he added, indicating a focus on public interest.To navigate the changing regulatory landscape, Milbank attorneys outlined practical steps for prediction market operators. Recommendations included evaluating existing contract listings for compliance, establishing relationships with sports leagues, assessing manipulation risks, participating in the rulemaking process, and staying informed about blockchain developments. Mainland also provided an overview of over 20 ongoing court cases related to prediction markets, indicating that the legal landscape remains complex and uncertain, especially regarding the question of whether the Commodity Exchange Act preempts state law.

+ MILBANK TO FACILITATE DISCUSSION FOR KENNEDY-WILSON BONDHOLDERS

Milbank LLP will host a call for holders of Kennedy-Wilson, Inc.'s 4.75% senior notes due 2029, 4.75% senior notes due 2030, and 5.00% senior notes due 2031. The call is set to take place today, 5th March 2026, at 3:30 p.m. (EST), addressing concerns raised by numerous bondholders regarding the recent Exchange Offer and consent solicitation statement dated 2nd March 2026.This call is particularly significant as Milbank has received indications of interest from holders of a substantial portion of the Notes, suggesting a heightened level of engagement among investors. Bondholders who wish to participate in the call must provide their detailed contact information, along with their holdings information on a series-by-series basis, to ensure their inclusion in the discussion.Milbank LLP, a prominent international law firm founded over 150 years ago in New York, specializes in providing innovative legal services to clients globally. With offices in key financial hubs including London, Hong Kong, and S o Paulo, Milbank collaborates across various practices to assist commercial, financial, and industrial enterprises, as well as institutions and governments, in achieving their strategic objectives.In addition to hosting calls for Kennedy-Wilson bondholders, Milbank has been actively analyzing recent developments affecting unsecured creditors of Braskem S.A. This highlights the firm’s commitment to providing timely and relevant legal advice to its clients in an ever-evolving financial landscape.As Milbank continues to expand its footprint and enhance its services, the firm remains dedicated to fostering transparent communication between issuers and investors, ensuring that concerns are addressed promptly and effectively.

value.Title: Amitav Ghosh, writer: 'The doomsday shelter industry in the United States has become very big'
value.Overview: El Pais - English, CLIMATE CHANGE NEWS & US NEWS, 225words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGR-KJV3-SBCB-N39K-00000-00Amitav Ghosh, writer: 'The doomsday shelter industry in the United States has become very big'2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisCLIMATE CHANGE NEWS & US NEWSAmitav Ghosh, writer: 'The doomsday shelter industry in the United States has become very big'Clemente Álvarez Andrés

Link to Image

Link to Image

Link to Image

Link to Image

Q. What alternative is there?

A. It must be said that China is very different from India, because it has led the way in renewable energy. And now it is installing more alternative energy plants than the rest of the world put together. As for the Western imitation effect, this offers hope: if the West were to change its way of life, if it were to embrace alternatives like those advocated by young people like Greta Thunberg, then people in other parts of the world would also want that way of life.

Q. So you have hope?

A. As Gramsci said, we must have pessimism of the intellect and optimism of the will. I think that's the only thing we can say at this point, because everything is heading in the wrong direction. The United States is in the grip of a crisis as it has never experienced before; no one has ever seen a polarization of the kind that exists today in the country. I think what really threatens Americans right now are the problems related to the standard of living. Today, the average age of a first-time homeowner in a city like New York or Chicago or San Francisco is around 50. And in Europe, where until now the climate issue was very important, they now only talk about rearming.

value.Title: Indian MBA student wins $20,000 Stanford prize for clean energy and emerging markets work
value.Overview: Sithara Rasheed, an MBA graduate of Stanford's Graduate School of Business , has been named one of four winners of the 2026 Stanford Impact Leader (SIL) Prize, a $20,000 award given annually to graduating students committed to careers addressing the world's most...

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJV-YWF3-RSGR-Y2FJ-00000-00Indian MBA student wins $20,000 Stanford prize for clean energy and emerging markets work2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisNEWSIndian MBA student wins $20,000 Stanford prize for clean energy and emerging markets work

Sithara Rasheed, an MBA graduate of Stanford's Graduate School of Business, has been named one of four winners of the 2026 Stanford Impact Leader (SIL) Prize, a $20,000 award given annually to graduating students committed to careers addressing the world's most...

Sithara Rasheed, an MBA graduate of Stanford's Graduate School of Business, has been named one of four winners of the 2026 Stanford Impact Leader (SIL) Prize, a $20,000 award given annually to graduating students committed to careers addressing the world's...

Sithara Rasheed, an MBA graduate of Stanford's Graduate School of Business, has been named one of four winners of the 2026 Stanford Impact Leader (SIL) Prize, a $20,000 award given annually to graduating students committed to careers addressing the world's most pressing challenges. Selected from a graduating class of approximately 430 students, Sithara was chosen for the prestigious prize, alongside classmates Evans Adanya, Anshul Dhingra, and Alexis Cook, by a panel of distinguished impact leaders.The recognition follows nearly a decade of work in climate finance and innovation that has shaped policy and funding decisions affecting communities across two continents. Before Stanford, Sithara spent five years as Manager of Innovation Investments at the Rockefeller Foundation in New York, where she built the Power and Climate Initiative's first-ever innovation portfolio, directly deploying over $40 million across Sub-Saharan Africa and Asia.Her work spanned both program design and direct investments, with measurable impact at scale. She designed and launched the DART program, which aggregated procurement for solar project developers across key African markets, driving down the costs of building and generating renewable power. She also designed and led the eGUIDE initiative, a flagship AI effort improving electricity planning decisions for grids and governments across East and West Africa - ultimately delivering faster, cheaper, and cleaner power to millions of people.On the investment side, she drove the Foundation's anchor commitment into Equator, a fund scaling high-impact clean energy ventures across Africa, and the Lacuna Fund, a collaborative initiative with Google.org, GIZ, and IDRC to develop open AI training datasets for underrepresented communities. Sithara has also represented this work at prominent global forums including COP, NY Climate Week, and Utility Innovation Week, advocating for the equitable deployment of high-impact climate technologies in emerging markets. In July 2024, she was part of the inaugural meeting of the Frontier Technology Coalition, held at the Bellagio Center.During her MBA summer at Artisan Partners in New York, she led the development of the Sustainable Emerging Markets Fund’s AI investment thesis.The Stanford Impact Leader (SIL) Prize is awarded annually by Stanford's Centre for Social Innovation (CSI) to graduating MBA students who demonstrate outstanding leadership, a long-term commitment to social impact, and a clear plan to address pressing global challenges. Stanford GSB, which reclaimed the number one ranking in the 2026 US News and World Report Best Business Schools list with an acceptance rate of 6.8 per cent, is widely regarded as the most selective MBA program in the United States.

APPENDIX II STOCK IDENTIFIERS

SECTION 1 CORPORATE PROFILE

1.1 Activities

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED) is a leading energy utility company headquartered in New York, serving over 3.5 million customers in New York City and Westchester County. Its core business activities encompass the transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity, natural gas, and steam, supporting residential, commercial, and industrial sectors with a focus on reliability and safety. The company operates through subsidiaries like Consolidated Edison Company of New York and Orange and Rockland Utilities, emphasizing infrastructure maintenance and regulatory compliance. Fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, as well as electric vehicle infrastructure and grid modernization initiatives, driven by rising demand for sustainable energy solutions and state-level clean energy mandates. These efforts have bolstered ConEd's revenue growth, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy while maintaining operational excellence. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 2 RECENT NEWS, PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

2.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 428 words

Tue May 12, 2026: Con Edison to Invest $29 Billion in NYC Grid Enhancement Amid Electrification Trends

Consolidated Edison, Inc. is set to invest over $29 billion in its electric infrastructure to enhance the grid serving New York City and its surrounding suburbs. This substantial spending plan comes as the company observes an increasing demand for electrification in its service area. The announcement was made during a first-quarter earnings presentation on the 7th of May, 2026, signaling a proactive approach to meet electrification needs that are being driven primarily by buildings and transportation rather than the data center boom seen in other regions.

The planned investment breaks down to approximately $27.2 billion for Consolidated Edison Co. of New York, commonly known as CECONY, and an additional $2.3 billion from Orange & Rockland Utilities over the next five years. This figures into a total capital spending strategy of about $38 billion that includes investments in gas and steam. Despite a slight decline in weather-adjusted electricity sales for CECONY—down 0.1% compared to the previous year—CEO Tim Cawley emphasized the company's commitment to investing in infrastructure to support future demand growth.

Cawley highlighted that the electrification of heating and transportation is accelerating, supported by state and local policies as well as customer preferences. In CECONY's service territory, new buildings are reportedly seeking 20% to 25% more power than their predecessors. Additionally, the region witnessed an 18% increase in fast-charging capacity for electric vehicles from the previous year. These developments are indicative of a broader trend where medium- and heavy-duty transportation electrification initiatives are fostering the adoption of electric trucks and buses.

In light of expected increases in demand, CECONY has identified the need for additional reliability measures. The New York Independent System Operator has raised concerns about tightening capacity margins, especially during peak summer months. Forecasts predict that CECONY will require an additional 125 MW by 2032 and 750 MW by 2036 to adequately meet demand as old fossil fuel generators retire and new projects face uncertainties. In response, CECONY has issued a request for information to explore cost-effective options to enhance grid reliability.

Con Edison is also pushing forward with its renewable energy initiatives, proposing a large-scale renewables ownership model that would enable the utility to procure and own renewable generation in New York State. This initiative, alongside a separate procurement process for renewable projects, demonstrates Con Edison's commitment to sustainability as it seeks to balance growth with environmental responsibility. As the company continues to adapt to the evolving energy landscape, its investments in infrastructure and renewable energy are poised to play a crucial role in ensuring a reliable and sustainable power supply for New York City.

2.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 89 words

Historic Rate Case Settlement Drastically Reduces Customer Bill Increases

The New York State Public Service Commission approved a three-year rate settlement for Consolidated Edison, significantly reducing proposed bill increases. Annual electric rate hikes were cut from 13.4% to 2.8%, and gas rates from 19% to 2%. The agreement, supported by multiple stakeholders, also includes provisions for improved language access, expanded low-income program outreach, and enhanced GHG accounting. Financially, the settlement provides revenue predictability for Con Edison's $38 billion capital plan through 2028, reducing regulatory risk for investors while addressing customer affordability, particularly for those transitioning to heat pumps.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 84 words

Q3 2025 Earnings Beat Consensus; 51-Year Dividend Aristocrat Continues Growth

Consolidated Edison reported strong Q3 2025 financial results, with adjusted earnings per share of $1.90, exceeding analyst expectations of $1.76. This performance was driven by increases in the company's regulated rate base and new rate plans. Con Edison subsequently raised its full-year earnings guidance and declared its quarterly dividend, continuing a 51-year streak of increases. The results were bolstered by $2.35 billion in recent infrastructure upgrades, reinforcing for investors the utility's reliable earnings power and its position as a defensive investment with predictable growth.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 86 words

$38 Billion Capital Investment Plan Approved; Targets 8.2% Annual Rate Base Growth Through 2029

Consolidated Edison has received regulatory approval for a $38 billion capital investment plan for 2025-2029, aimed at modernizing the grid, enhancing reliability, and supporting New York's clean energy goals. The plan is projected to drive an 8.2% compound annual growth rate in the company's rate base, providing long-term financial visibility. A transparent equity issuance plan is in place to fund the investments. For investors, this approval confirms regulatory support for the company's strategic infrastructure projects, which underpin its financial stability and capacity for sustainable dividend growth.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 89 words

NYISO Issues Critical Grid Reliability Warning; NYC Faces Dangerously Thin Reliability Margins Starting 2026

The New York Independent System Operator (NYISO) has warned that New York City will face a high risk of service disruptions starting in summer 2026 due to dangerously thin reliability margins. The issue stems from rising electricity demand, generator retirements, and transmission limits. NYISO identified several key projects, including the Champlain Hudson Power Express and Con Edison's Reliable Clean City projects, as essential to preventing reliability violations. This warning validates the strategic need for Con Edison's infrastructure investments and strengthens its position for rate recovery on these critical projects.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 87 words

Reliable Clean City Transmission Projects Add 900 MW Capacity Across NYC Boroughs

Consolidated Edison has advanced its Reliable Clean City program, an $800 million initiative that has added 900 MW of new transmission capacity across Queens, Brooklyn, and Staten Island. These projects are crucial for integrating renewable energy and enabling the retirement of older fossil fuel plants, supporting New York's 2040 zero-emission electricity goal. The on-schedule, on-budget execution of these projects, identified by NYISO as critical for grid reliability, enhances investor confidence in Con Edison's ability to manage its larger capital plan and supports its projected rate base growth.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 84 words

Brooklyn Clean Energy Hub Advances Offshore Wind Integration; 6 GW Capacity Target Established

Consolidated Edison is developing the Brooklyn Clean Energy Hub, an $810 million project designed to integrate up to 6,000 megawatts (6 GW) of offshore wind power into NYC's grid. The hub will create a new substation to address transmission bottlenecks, facilitating New York's goal of procuring 9,000 MW of offshore wind by 2035. The project has received regulatory approval and is designed with resilience features to serve disadvantaged communities. For investors, it represents a long-term, revenue-generating asset with strong regulatory backing and predictable returns.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 85 words

Champlain Hudson Power Express on Track for May 2026 In-Service; 1,250 MW Clean Hydropower Import Facility Nears Completion

The Champlain Hudson Power Express (CHPE), a $4.5 billion transmission line, is on schedule for its May 2026 in-service date. The project will transmit 1,250 MW of clean hydropower from Quebec to New York City, addressing the city's impending grid reliability issues. Identified by NYISO as a "permanent solution" to near-term needs, CHPE is expected to reduce electricity costs and CO2 emissions. For Con Edison, the project's completion is integral to its broader strategy of integrating large-scale renewable energy, reducing risk and boosting investor confidence.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Record $2.35 Billion Summer Infrastructure Investment Enhances Reliability and Electrification Readiness

Consolidated Edison has invested $2.35 billion in electric delivery system upgrades across its service area to prepare for rising summer demand and long-term electrification growth. The investments included new transformers, cables, and substation equipment, strategically targeting areas with high projected demand. These upgrades, executed while maintaining service reliability, support the company's projected 8.2% rate base growth and provide the regulatory basis for rate recovery, demonstrating to investors the company's capacity for effective capital deployment.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 77 words

Consolidated Edison Company of New York Issues $900 Million Long-Term Debt; $500 Million Term Loan Refinances Near-Term Obligations

In November 2025, Con Edison's subsidiary, CECONY, raised $1.4 billion in debt to fund its capital program. This included $900 million in 30-year debentures at a 5.75% coupon and a $500 million short-term loan to refinance existing obligations. The successful financing demonstrates strong market confidence in CECONY's credit quality and regulated business model. This disciplined, staggered approach to financing provides capital structure flexibility while securing long-term funding for infrastructure investments projected to drive significant rate base growth.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 79 words

Energy Affordability Program Delivers Record $311 Million in Low-Income Customer Discounts; 408,000-466,000 Customers Served

In 2024, Con Edison provided a record $311 million in bill discounts to over 408,000 low-income customers through its Energy Affordability Program (EAP), a 17% increase from 2023. The program, which aims to cap energy bills at 6% of household income, is expanding in 2026 to include more moderate-income households. This initiative demonstrates the company's commitment to customer affordability amid its large-scale infrastructure investments, enhancing its regulatory relationships and bolstering investor confidence in the stability of its operating environment.

2.3 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 353 words

May 8, 2026: AEP's $78 Billion Capex Highlights Industry Cost Pressures Indirectly Affecting Consolidated Edison

American Electric Power (Nasdaq: AEP) disclosed on May 5, 2026 an expansion of its five-year capital plan to $78 billion from $72 billion, alongside an upward revision of its incremental load forecast to 63 gigawatts by 2030. Approximately 90% of that projected demand is associated with hyperscaler and data center customers, according to company disclosures. Following 7 GW of new large-load agreements executed in the first quarter, concentrated in Ohio and Texas, the company indicated that AEP Texas alone accounts for 41 GW of incremental commitments, with Texas Senate Bill 6 expected to enhance interconnection certainty.

The expanded capital program is anchored by newly approved transmission investments in PJM and SPP, complemented by new natural gas-fired generation in Indiana. The company also indicated line of sight to more than $10 billion in additional investment beyond the current plan, and disclosed cost offsets of up to $16 billion for existing customers driven by signed customer agreements.

The announcement reflects how AI-driven data center load is reshaping regulated utility capital programs across the PJM, SPP, and ERCOT footprints.

Impact on Consolidated Edison: ED

Following this announcement, Consolidated Edison faces a more muted strategic read-across given its New York City and Westchester service territory, where data center concentration is materially lower than in Virginia, Ohio, Texas, or Georgia. Con Edison's capital plan is dominated by grid modernization, gas system safety, and clean energy transition investments mandated by New York State's Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act.

AEP's announcement still carries indirect implications. Industry-wide supply chain tightness for transformers, switchgear, and skilled trades, driven by the cumulative scale of AEP's, Dominion's, and Duke's programs, will affect costs and lead times for Con Edison's own infrastructure replacement and electrification investments. Con Edison's regulatory framework with the New York Public Service Commission allows for recovery of grid investments through rate cases, and rising input costs from the broader utility capex cycle will need to be reflected in those filings.

Con Edison will absorb the cost-side consequences of an industry-wide buildout in which it does not directly participate.

ECO NEWS 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 230 words

Apr 22, 2026: Anterix Closes Benton PUD 900 MHz Spectrum Deal, Extending Utility Private-LTE Ecosystem Relevant to Consolidated Edison's Urban Grid Modernization Plans

On April 21, 2026, Anterix Inc. disclosed that its subsidiary PDV Spectrum Holding Company, LLC, entered into a spectrum license sale agreement with Public Utility District No. 1 of Benton County for a 10 MHz 900 MHz broadband license in Washington. According to industry reports, the agreement, valued at $771,000, supports a utility-owned network spanning 927 square miles and serving more than 58,000 customers.

Market data indicates the transaction represents the first private wireless broadband deployment among publicly owned utilities in the Pacific Northwest. The deployment supports grid automation, field connectivity, and outage response.

Impact on Consolidated Edison: ED

Following this development, Consolidated Edison gains another peer-utility reference deployment relevant to communications infrastructure planning across its New York City and Westchester County service territories. Operational metrics indicate Con Edison serves approximately 3.6 million electric, 1.1 million gas, and 1,500 steam customers in one of the most complex and densely populated urban utility environments in the U.S.

Analysis shows the Benton PUD agreement extends the national base of deployed utility 900 MHz private LTE networks, maturing the vendor ecosystem and operational playbooks relevant to any private wireless investment Con Edison might evaluate as part of its grid hardening and electrification programs aligned with New York's Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act.

SECTION 3 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED) is a leading energy utility company headquartered in New York, serving over 3.5 million customers in New York City and Westchester County. Its core business activities encompass the transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity, natural gas, and steam, supporting residential, commercial, and industrial sectors with a focus on reliability and safety. The company operates through subsidiaries like Consolidated Edison Company of New York and Orange and Rockland Utilities, emphasizing infrastructure maintenance and regulatory compliance. Fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, as well as electric vehicle infrastructure and grid modernization initiatives, driven by rising demand for sustainable energy solutions and state-level clean energy mandates. These efforts have bolstered ConEd's revenue growth, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy while maintaining operational excellence. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

November 06 2025: CON EDISON REPORTS 2025 THIRD QUARTER EARNINGS

NEW YORK, Nov. 6, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Consolidated Edison, Inc. (Con Edison) (NYSE: ED) today reported 2025 third quarter net income for common stock of $688 million or $1.91 a share compared with $588 million or $1.70 a share in the 2024 third quarter. Adjusted earnings (non-GAAP) were $686 million or $1.90 a share in the 2025 period compared with $583 million or $1.68 a share in the 2024 period. Adjusted earnings and adjusted earnings per share in the 2025 and 2024 periods exclude accretion of the basis difference of Con Edison's equity investment in Mountain Valley Pipeline, LLC (MVP). Adjusted earnings and adjusted earnings per share in the 2024 period exclude the effects of hypothetical liquidation at book value (HLBV) accounting for tax equity investments.

For the first nine months of 2025, net income for common stock was $1,726 million or $4.84 a share compared with $1,510 million or $4.37 a share in the first nine months of 2024. 3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 89 words

Historic Rate Case Settlement Drastically Reduces Customer Bill Increases

The New York State Public Service Commission approved a three-year rate settlement for Consolidated Edison, significantly reducing proposed bill increases. Annual electric rate hikes were cut from 13.4% to 2.8%, and gas rates from 19% to 2%. The agreement, supported by multiple stakeholders, also includes provisions for improved language access, expanded low-income program outreach, and enhanced GHG accounting. Financially, the settlement provides revenue predictability for Con Edison's $38 billion capital plan through 2028, reducing regulatory risk for investors while addressing customer affordability, particularly for those transitioning to heat pumps.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 84 words

Q3 2025 Earnings Beat Consensus; 51-Year Dividend Aristocrat Continues Growth

Consolidated Edison reported strong Q3 2025 financial results, with adjusted earnings per share of $1.90, exceeding analyst expectations of $1.76. This performance was driven by increases in the company's regulated rate base and new rate plans. Con Edison subsequently raised its full-year earnings guidance and declared its quarterly dividend, continuing a 51-year streak of increases. The results were bolstered by $2.35 billion in recent infrastructure upgrades, reinforcing for investors the utility's reliable earnings power and its position as a defensive investment with predictable growth.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 86 words

$38 Billion Capital Investment Plan Approved; Targets 8.2% Annual Rate Base Growth Through 2029

Consolidated Edison has received regulatory approval for a $38 billion capital investment plan for 2025-2029, aimed at modernizing the grid, enhancing reliability, and supporting New York's clean energy goals. The plan is projected to drive an 8.2% compound annual growth rate in the company's rate base, providing long-term financial visibility. A transparent equity issuance plan is in place to fund the investments. For investors, this approval confirms regulatory support for the company's strategic infrastructure projects, which underpin its financial stability and capacity for sustainable dividend growth.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 89 words

NYISO Issues Critical Grid Reliability Warning; NYC Faces Dangerously Thin Reliability Margins Starting 2026

The New York Independent System Operator (NYISO) has warned that New York City will face a high risk of service disruptions starting in summer 2026 due to dangerously thin reliability margins. The issue stems from rising electricity demand, generator retirements, and transmission limits. NYISO identified several key projects, including the Champlain Hudson Power Express and Con Edison's Reliable Clean City projects, as essential to preventing reliability violations. This warning validates the strategic need for Con Edison's infrastructure investments and strengthens its position for rate recovery on these critical projects.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 87 words

Reliable Clean City Transmission Projects Add 900 MW Capacity Across NYC Boroughs

Consolidated Edison has advanced its Reliable Clean City program, an $800 million initiative that has added 900 MW of new transmission capacity across Queens, Brooklyn, and Staten Island. These projects are crucial for integrating renewable energy and enabling the retirement of older fossil fuel plants, supporting New York's 2040 zero-emission electricity goal. The on-schedule, on-budget execution of these projects, identified by NYISO as critical for grid reliability, enhances investor confidence in Con Edison's ability to manage its larger capital plan and supports its projected rate base growth.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 84 words

Brooklyn Clean Energy Hub Advances Offshore Wind Integration; 6 GW Capacity Target Established

Consolidated Edison is developing the Brooklyn Clean Energy Hub, an $810 million project designed to integrate up to 6,000 megawatts (6 GW) of offshore wind power into NYC's grid. The hub will create a new substation to address transmission bottlenecks, facilitating New York's goal of procuring 9,000 MW of offshore wind by 2035. The project has received regulatory approval and is designed with resilience features to serve disadvantaged communities. For investors, it represents a long-term, revenue-generating asset with strong regulatory backing and predictable returns.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 85 words

Champlain Hudson Power Express on Track for May 2026 In-Service; 1,250 MW Clean Hydropower Import Facility Nears Completion

The Champlain Hudson Power Express (CHPE), a $4.5 billion transmission line, is on schedule for its May 2026 in-service date. The project will transmit 1,250 MW of clean hydropower from Quebec to New York City, addressing the city's impending grid reliability issues. Identified by NYISO as a "permanent solution" to near-term needs, CHPE is expected to reduce electricity costs and CO2 emissions. For Con Edison, the project's completion is integral to its broader strategy of integrating large-scale renewable energy, reducing risk and boosting investor confidence.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Record $2.35 Billion Summer Infrastructure Investment Enhances Reliability and Electrification Readiness

Consolidated Edison has invested $2.35 billion in electric delivery system upgrades across its service area to prepare for rising summer demand and long-term electrification growth. The investments included new transformers, cables, and substation equipment, strategically targeting areas with high projected demand. These upgrades, executed while maintaining service reliability, support the company's projected 8.2% rate base growth and provide the regulatory basis for rate recovery, demonstrating to investors the company's capacity for effective capital deployment.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 77 words

Consolidated Edison Company of New York Issues $900 Million Long-Term Debt; $500 Million Term Loan Refinances Near-Term Obligations

In November 2025, Con Edison's subsidiary, CECONY, raised $1.4 billion in debt to fund its capital program. This included $900 million in 30-year debentures at a 5.75% coupon and a $500 million short-term loan to refinance existing obligations. The successful financing demonstrates strong market confidence in CECONY's credit quality and regulated business model. This disciplined, staggered approach to financing provides capital structure flexibility while securing long-term funding for infrastructure investments projected to drive significant rate base growth.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 79 words

Energy Affordability Program Delivers Record $311 Million in Low-Income Customer Discounts; 408,000-466,000 Customers Served

In 2024, Con Edison provided a record $311 million in bill discounts to over 408,000 low-income customers through its Energy Affordability Program (EAP), a 17% increase from 2023. The program, which aims to cap energy bills at 6% of household income, is expanding in 2026 to include more moderate-income households. This initiative demonstrates the company's commitment to customer affordability amid its large-scale infrastructure investments, enhancing its regulatory relationships and bolstering investor confidence in the stability of its operating environment.

3.3 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 230 words

Apr 22, 2026: Anterix Closes Benton PUD 900 MHz Spectrum Deal, Extending Utility Private-LTE Ecosystem Relevant to Consolidated Edison's Urban Grid Modernization Plans

On April 21, 2026, Anterix Inc. disclosed that its subsidiary PDV Spectrum Holding Company, LLC, entered into a spectrum license sale agreement with Public Utility District No. 1 of Benton County for a 10 MHz 900 MHz broadband license in Washington. According to industry reports, the agreement, valued at $771,000, supports a utility-owned network spanning 927 square miles and serving more than 58,000 customers.

Market data indicates the transaction represents the first private wireless broadband deployment among publicly owned utilities in the Pacific Northwest. The deployment supports grid automation, field connectivity, and outage response.

Impact on Consolidated Edison: ED

Following this development, Consolidated Edison gains another peer-utility reference deployment relevant to communications infrastructure planning across its New York City and Westchester County service territories. Operational metrics indicate Con Edison serves approximately 3.6 million electric, 1.1 million gas, and 1,500 steam customers in one of the most complex and densely populated urban utility environments in the U.S.

Analysis shows the Benton PUD agreement extends the national base of deployed utility 900 MHz private LTE networks, maturing the vendor ecosystem and operational playbooks relevant to any private wireless investment Con Edison might evaluate as part of its grid hardening and electrification programs aligned with New York's Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act.

value.Title: Eco Wave Power Announced Participation in the D. Boral Capital Global Conference

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-Y113-S5Y1-60HM-00000-00Eco Wave Power Announced Participation in the D. Boral Capital Global Conference2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisEco Wave Power Announced Participation in the D. Boral Capital Global Conference

New York: Eco Wave Power Global AB (publ) (NASDAQ: WAVE) (Eco Wave Poweror the Company), a leading onshore wave energy technology company, today announced its participation in the D. Boral Capital Global Conference, taking place on May 7, 2026, at The Plaza Hotel in New York City.

Eco Wave Power's Founder and Chief Executive Officer, Inna Braverman, will host one-on-one meetings with investors and other interested parties on May 7, 2026, from 9:45 a.m to 2:45 p.m ET.

During the meetings, Ms. Braverman is expected to discuss Eco Wave Power's recent operational progress, including the Company's grid-connected wave energy power station in Israel and its pilot project at the Port ofLos Angeles, launched in collaboration with Shell Marine Renewable Energy. She is also expected to provide updates on the Company's expanding international growth pipeline, including planned projects in Taiwan, India, and Portugal.

In addition, discussions may address Eco Wave Power's potential role in supporting next-generation digital and industrial infrastructure by serving as a renewable energy layer for AI-related facilities, ports, and energy-intensive assets located near coastlines.
