value.Title: Freeport-McMoRan Could Benefit from Copper Export Activity as US Current Account Deficit Narrows

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-XK43-SHD0-K4MH-00000-00Freeport-McMoRan Could Benefit from Copper Export Activity as US Current Account Deficit Narrows2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisFreeport-McMoRan Could Benefit from Copper Export Activity as US Current Account Deficit Narrows

NYSE STOCK REPORT

The U.S. current account deficit narrowed to $226.4 billion in the third quarter of 2025 from $249.2 billion in the second quarter, reducing the gap to 2.9% of gross domestic product from 3.3% and undershooting market expectations of $238 billion. The improvement was driven by a turnaround in primary income from deficit to surplus as American companies increased foreign earnings, together with an expanded services surplus and a modest decrease in the goods deficit, after a first quarter when the deficit hit $450.2 billion as businesses rushed to import goods ahead of new tariffs.

Impact on Freeport-McMoRan Inc.

The primary income turnaround could benefit Freeport-McMoRan Inc., which operates large-scale mining assets in Indonesia and South America that generate reinvested earnings recorded in the direct investment income category. Copper concentrate produced at its overseas mines and exported globally contributes to industrial supplies trade flows, while the company also runs U.S. mines and smelters, with copper exports adding to the goods trade balance, and growing global demand for copper driven by electrification and AI infrastructure buildouts could support both export activity and overseas earnings, with a weaker dollar potentially enhancing the competitiveness of U.S.-sourced copper.

A year ago the Freeport- McMoRan Inc. share price was $38.88. $1,000 would have bought 25.7 Freeport- McMoRan Inc. shares on that day. Those 25.7 Shares would be worth $1,698 at today's share price of $66.03. PV$1000= $1,698.

Activities

Freeport-McMoRan Inc. is a leading U.S.-based mining company specializing in the exploration, development, and production of copper, gold, and molybdenum. Headquartered in Phoenix, Arizona, the company operates large-scale mines in the Americas and Indonesia, leveraging advanced technologies to extract and process these essential metals for global industries such as electronics, construction, and renewable energy. Its core business focuses on sustainable resource management, emphasizing operational efficiency and environmental stewardship to meet rising demand. The fastest-growing segments include copper production, which has expanded rapidly due to increasing needs in electric vehicles and infrastructure projects, as well as gold operations that have benefited from higher prices and new mine developments. Overall, Freeport-McMoRan's strategic investments in high-potential assets position it as a key player in the global metals market, driving long-term value through diversified revenue streams and innovation. It is the NYSE's largest Non ferrous metals sector company by market capitalisation.

Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (NYSE:FCX) is a leading copper mining company and major copper producer Freeport, known for its extensive operations in Freeport copper production. With distinguished assets such as the Grasberg copper mine, Indonesia copper mines Freeport, and North American copper mines Freeport, the company plays a pivotal role in the global industrial copper supply FCX. Notably, its mining activities encompass open-pit copper mining Freeport and sulfide copper processing Freeport, leveraging porphyry copper deposits to maintain its vast Freeport mineral reserves. Apart from Freeport-McMoRan copper, FCX is a significant gold producer Freeport with involvement in gold mining FCX. As a molybdenum producer Freeport, it yields essential commodities like molybdenum concentrate FCX. An emphasis on sustainable mining Freeport and ESG Freeport-McMoRan aligns with market trends. The FCX stock reflects these robust fundamentals, and mining stocks copper exposure is key for those investing in Freeport-McMoRan considering the copper price impact on FCX and FCX dividend yield, bolstered by a comprehensive Freeport production report and diligent management of the Freeport mining assets portfolio.

value.Title: US pension fund threatens to divest TotalEnergies stake over offshore wind exit
value.Overview: One of the largest US pension funds is re-evaluating its stake in TotalEnergies following the French oil major’s decision to accept $1bn fro...

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHN-9H83-S199-T147-00000-00US pension fund threatens to divest TotalEnergies stake over offshore wind exit2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisUS pension fund threatens to divest TotalEnergies stake over offshore wind exitMartha Muir and Jamie Smyth in New York

One of the largest US pension funds is re-evaluating its stake in TotalEnergies following the French oil major’s decision to accept $1bn fro...

One of the largest US pension funds is re-evaluating its stake in TotalEnergies following the French oil major’s decision to accept $1bn from Donald Trump’s administration to exit offshore wind power in the US.

The New York State Common Retirement Fund, which owns a $1.6mn stake in the oil major, told Total its decision to terminate two offshore wind leases and redirect investment into fossil fuels raised “significant concerns” regarding its strategic consistency, financial discipline and risk management, according to a letter to Total chief executive Patrick Pouyanné seen by the FT.

While the fund’s stake in Total is small and divestiture would be symbolic, the warning indicates growing resistance to the White House’s use of lease refunds to stop offshore wind projects. It also creates a bind for energy companies caught between competing political forces in the US, with Republicans trying to block renewable energy and Democrats promoting it.

It comes after the California Energy Commission issued a subpoena to Golden State Wind on Wednesday, seeking information about the company’s $120mn agreement with the Trump administration to end its offshore wind lease.

In March the US interior department said it would pay Total $928mn, the cost of two leases it purchased to develop offshore wind projects in US waters off the coasts of New York and North Carolina. Total will instead invest the money in fossil-fuel projects.

The department wants to make further deals with companies that hold offshore wind leases, saying it has “held talks with several companies with offshore wind leases to persuade them to enact deals such as the one agreed with TotalEnergies”.

In the letter to Pouyanné, Thomas DiNapoli, the New York state comptroller and trustee of the retirement fund said: “As the fund continually evaluates companies based on credible transition plans, portfolio companies’ backtracking may impact the fund’s risk assessment results and proxy voting decisions.”

Total’s deal with the administration “raises risks for the company and its investors”, DiNapoli said. “TotalEnergies had sought to be a leader in energy transition, now investors are left scratching their heads over how the board came to this decision to abandon that strategy and what it means for the future of the company and our stake in it.”

Total did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Trump has made stopping the offshore wind industry a major pillar of his second term, issuing multiple stop-work orders and citing national security concerns to shut down construction on projects. These attempts have proved unsuccessful in federal courts, leading to direct negotiations between the White House and the companies to scrap the leases.

DiNapoli’s letter pushes for clarity on how Total assessed “the legal and regulatory risks” of accepting the payment, and the risk it could be overturned under new political leadership.

The fund, which has $298bn in assets under management as of February 2026, says it evaluates company directors’ actions on climate through proxy voting as part of its effort to address climate change.

Total has pledged to produce 120 terawatt hours of mostly wind and solar-generated power by 2030 and for 75 per cent of its energy mix to be low carbon by 2050.

urn:contentItem:6JHD-58Y3-S4MW-D33P-00000-00MN8 Energy Closes Tolling Agreement, Expands Public EV Charging Footprint in San Jose, California2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisNEWSMN8 Energy Closes Tolling Agreement, Expands Public EV Charging Footprint in San Jose, California

Publish Your PressRelease 6 May 2026 MN8 Energy LLC (“MN8”) today announced a new tolling agreement with San Jose Clean Energy (“SJCE”) to design, build, own, and operate 40 direct-current fast-charging (DCFC) ports across five city-owned sites. Under...

Publish Your PressRelease 6 May 2026 MN8 Energy LLC (“MN8”) today announced a new tolling agreement with San Jose Clean Energy (“SJCE”) to design, build, own, and operate 40 direct-current fast-charging (DCFC) ports across five city-owned sites. Under the ...

Publish Your PressRelease 6 May 2026 MN8 Energy LLC (“MN8”) today announced a new tolling agreement with San Jose Clean Energy (“SJCE”) to design, build, own, and operate 40 direct-current fast-charging (DCFC) ports across five city-owned sites. Under the agreement, MN8 is responsible for funding project costs and supporting operational performance, while SJCE retains control over branding and retail pricing. Revenue is collected and reconciled against a fixed fee structure, creating a predictable framework for both parties. The novel tolling agreement is modeled after the way California utilities and CCAs currently procure power assets, removing the complexity of acquiring EV charging infrastructure. The model eliminates ownership risks for public agencies, lowering the barrier to deployment. “This is EV charging procurement done the way the industry already knows how to work,” said Alan Dowdell, Head of Distributed Energy Solutions (DES) at MN8 Energy. “We’ve built this model to work for any CCA looking to procure EV charging. San Jose is our first stop, we’re excited to continue building across the state.” MN8’s DES platform brings a proven track record of providing EV charging infrastructure in partnership with cities and in service of underserved communities. In July 2025, the company announced its Gateway to Hunts Point project, a first-of-its-kind freight EV charging hub in New York’s South Bronx. The project reflects MN8’s commitment to deploying infrastructure investment in underserved communities. The deployment in San Jose covers five city-owned sites, totaling 8 MW of capacity and featuring 20 dual-port chargers across neighborhoods with limited access to public EV charging infrastructure. Construction is expected to begin in late 2026, with stations coming online in early 2027. About MN8 MN8 Energy serves enterprise customers on their journey to an electrified, decarbonized world by providing renewable energy and related services. With approximately 4 gigawatts of operational and under-construction solar projects, 1.1 gigawatt hours of battery energy storage capacity comprised of over 874 projects across 29 states, and more than 100 high-power EV charging stations across 26 states, MN8 is one of the largest and most sophisticated independent solar energy and energy storage power producers in the United States. MN8 is headquartered in New York and has offices in South Florida, Dallas, and Madrid. Learn more at MN8.com. Contacts Julia Dodig julia.dodig@mn8.com +1-574-607-5474 Source; businesswire.com Report Abuse

value.Title: How Diamondback Energy Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-YV13-SHN4-41CT-00000-00How Diamondback Energy Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisHow Diamondback Energy Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

During President Trump's second term, corporate investment in the United States has increased, with major companies in technology, manufacturing, and healthcare allocating billions to domestic expansion. The semiconductor industry has committed over $500 billion in private-sector funding, indicating a notable reshoring trend. This investment activity is backed by enhanced tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, including 100% bonus depreciation and 35% manufacturing credits, and tariff policies aimed at supporting domestic production.

The reshoring trend affects multiple high-value sectors. Semiconductor manufacturing investments of over $500 billion across U.S. facilities are expected to create more than 150,000 new jobs, while pharmaceutical companies have allocated over $150 billion for U.S. manufacturing investments. Foreign direct investment was recorded at $102 billion in Q2 2025, representing a 137% increase from Q1. This comprehensive restructuring is influenced by both policy incentives and supply chain resilience priorities.

This report examines investment commitments from companies across the technology, manufacturing, healthcare, and energy sectors.

Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: FANG)

- Company Profile: Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: FANG) is a U.S.-based oil and gas firm concentrated on Permian Basin operations, employing approximately 900 people globally.

- Announced Investment: $3.8 billion in a 2025 capital program for drilling and completion activities in the Permian Basin, increasing production capacity.

- Recipient: Diamondback Energy U.S. Permian Operations.

- Industries Impacted: Energy, Oil and Gas.

Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ: BKR)

- Company Profile: Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ: BKR) is a U.S.-based energy technology firm that supplies oilfield services and equipment, with about 55,000 employees globally.

- Announced Investment: $1.5 billion is allocated for 2025-2026 to advance carbon capture and hydrogen technology, and expand U.S. manufacturing for energy transition equipment.

- Recipient: Baker Hughes U.S. Energy Transition Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Energy Technology, Manufacturing

Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE: LNG)

- Company Profile: Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE: LNG) is a U.S.-based LNG firm that runs liquefaction and export facilities, employing approximately 1,800 people globally.

- Announced Investment: A $4 billion investment for 2025-2026 is aimed at expanding the Sabine Pass and Corpus Christi LNG facilities to increase export capacity.

- Recipient: Cheniere Energy U.S. LNG Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Energy, LNG Export

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED)

- Company Profile: Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED) is a U.S.-based utility that provides electric, gas, and steam services, with a global employee count of approximately 15,000.

- Announced Investment: $2.8 billion planned for 2025-2026 to upgrade the New York City electric grid, improve resilience to weather, and increase renewable energy connections.

- Recipient: Consolidated Edison U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

Crown Castle International (NYSE: CCI)

- Company Profile: Crown Castle International (NYSE: CCI) is a U.S.-based telecommunications infrastructure firm that manages towers and fiber networks, with a team of approximately 7,000.

- Announced Investment: $3.5 billion for 2025-2026 to build out fiber-to-the-premises networks and 5G infrastructure throughout the U.S.

- Recipient: Crown Castle U.S. Telecom Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Telecommunications, Infrastructure.

Deere and Company

- Company Profile: Deere and Company (NYSE: DE) is a firm specializing in agricultural machinery and equipment manufacturing, employing approximately 82,000 people worldwide.

- Announced Investment: $2 billion in 2025 to enlarge Illinois and Iowa manufacturing facilities for the production of advanced machinery.

- Recipient: Deere U.S. Manufacturing Plants.

- Industries Impacted: Manufacturing, Agriculture.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE: DTE)

- Company Profile: DTE Energy Company (NYSE: DTE) is a U.S.-based utility firm that supplies electric and gas services, employing approximately 10,000 people worldwide.

- Announced Investment: $2.3 billion for 2025-2026 to increase renewable energy capacity, update the Michigan electric grid, and develop natural gas infrastructure.

- Recipient: DTE Energy U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ: EXC)

- Company Profile: Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ: EXC) is a U.S.-based utility company that operates electric and gas utilities, with a global workforce of approximately 33,000.

- Announced Investment: $4.5 billion for 2025-2026 to modernize electric grid infrastructure, improve reliability, and increase clean energy integration in its service territories.

- Recipient: Exelon U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

FirstEnergy Corp. (NYSE: FE)

- Company Profile: FirstEnergy Corp. is an energy infrastructure operator with a workforce of approximately 15,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A multi-year investment of $15 billion is designated for infrastructure upgrades and grid modernization projects.

- Recipient: FirstEnergy U.S. Energy Grid

- Industries Impacted: Energy

First Solar Inc. (NASDAQ: FSLR)

- Company Profile: First Solar Inc., a U.S.-headquartered manufacturer of solar panels, maintains production facilities worldwide and employs about 6,400 people.

- Announced Investment: An allocation of $1.1 billion in 2025 will fund the construction of a new solar panel manufacturing plant in Ohio. A separate $1.1 billion, 3.5 GW facility in Louisiana is scheduled to be operational in late 2025.

- Recipient: First Solar U.S. Solar Panel Plants

- Industries Impacted: Renewable Energy, Manufacturing

Occidental Petroleum (NYSE: OXY)

- Company Profile: A U.S. energy company with a workforce of approximately 12,000 employees.

- Announced Investment: A $1.8 billion investment is planned for July 2025 for Texas enhanced oil recovery and direct air capture (DAC) plants.

- Recipient: U.S. energy sites

- Industries Impacted: Energy

ONEOK Inc. (NYSE: OKE)

- Company Profile: ONEOK, Inc., a U.S.-based energy company, manages extensive natural gas pipeline networks and employs approximately 3,000 individuals.

- Announced Investment: In August 2025, ONEOK confirmed its involvement in the Eiger Express Pipeline, a $1.2 billion, An error occurred during generation.

- Industries Impacted: Energy

Announced Investment: In August 2025, ONEOK confirmed its involvement in the Eiger Express Pipeline, a $1.2 billion, An error occurred during generation.Industries Impacted: Energy

Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE: PEG)

- Company Profile: Public Service Enterprise Group is a U.S.-based energy firm managing utilities and power generation, employing approximately 13,000 people globally.

- Announced Investment: A sum of $3.2 billion is designated for 2025-2026 to build offshore wind infrastructure, update transmission systems, and increase clean energy generation in New Jersey.

- Recipient: Public Service Enterprise Group U.S. Energy Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Renewable Energy

Rollins Inc. (NYSE: ROL)

- Company Profile: Rollins Inc. (NYSE: ROL) is a U.S.-headquartered pest control and termite services firm, with a global workforce of around 18,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A $1 billion capital injection in 2025 is allocated for fleet modernization and facility expansions.

- Recipient: Rollins U.S. Operations Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Services, Facilities

Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT)

- Company Profile: Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT), a prominent U.S.-headquartered retailer, manages stores and distribution networks with a workforce of around 440,000 employees.

- Announced Investment: A $1 billion development plan for 2025 is intended to improve distribution centers in Minnesota to accommodate e-commerce expansion.

- Recipient: Target U.S. Retail Operations

- Industries Impacted: Retail, Logistics

A year ago the Diamondback Energy, Inc. share price was $129.54. $1,000 would have bought 7.7 Diamondback Energy, Inc. shares on that day. Those 7.7 Shares would be worth $1,592 at today's share price of $206.18. PV$1000= $1,592.

Activities

Diamondback Energy, Inc., a leading independent oil and natural gas exploration and production company based in Midland, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, primarily operates in the Permian Basin of West Texas and New Mexico. The company's core activities involve acquiring, developing, and producing hydrocarbons, with a strong emphasis on advanced techniques like horizontal drilling and hydraulic fracturing to extract oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids from shale formations. Among its fastest-growing segments are the Delaware Basin operations, which have seen significant production increases due to strategic acquisitions, such as the 2021 merger with Endeavor Energy Resources, boosting output of high-value oil and liquids. Similarly, the Midland Basin is expanding rapidly through enhanced recovery methods and infrastructure investments, driving overall volume growth amid rising energy demand. This focus on efficient resource development has solidified Diamondback's position as a key player in the U.S. shale market, supporting sustained revenue and operational expansion. It is the NASDAQ's largest Energy company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JHD-MY63-S5CS-V54N-00000-00Cooley Launches Energy Group With Baker Botts Partner2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisNEWSCooley Launches Energy Group With Baker Botts PartnerAndrea Keckley

Cooley LLP announced Wednesday that it is launching an infrastructure, energy and real estate group with a New York partner from Baker Botts LLP who advises on global energy and infrastructure projects. Mona Dajani will lead the group alongside longtime real...

Cooley LLP announced Wednesday that it is launching an infrastructure, energy and real estate group with a New York partner from Baker Botts LLP who advises on global energy and infrastructure projects. Mona Dajani will lead the group alongside longtime re...

Cooley LLP announced Wednesday that it is launching an infrastructure, energy and real estate group with a New York partner from Baker Botts LLP who advises on global energy and infrastructure projects. Mona Dajani will lead the group alongside longtime real estate and data center partner Michelle Schulman. She previously worked as the global co-chair of the energy, infrastructure and hydrogen practice at Baker Botts, which she joined in 2023 from Shearman & Sterling LLP. She is no stranger to practice leadership, having previously headed multiple energy-related teams at Shearman, as well as the renewable energy and infrastructure practice at Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP. "This is very much a market-driven move," Dajani told Law360 Pulse in an email Wednesday. "Cooley sits at a unique intersection of capital formation, infrastructure and real estate, working with sponsors, technology companies and capital providers, which gives us a clear line of sight into where energy demand is accelerating." The appointment comes as more firms look to invest in energy- and infrastructure-related offerings. Paul WeissRifkind Wharton & Garrison LLP, for instance, opened an office in Houston in February to focus on energy-related M&A and infrastructure matters. And in April, Taft Stettinius & Hollister LLP tapped an energy, infrastructure and project finance group from Steptoe LLP to lead the launch of its first office in New York City. "Mona adds invaluable expertise to our growing platform," Rachel Proffitt, partner and CEO of Cooley, said in a statement. "The infrastructure market is converging where our clients are focused and where the market is heading: toward complex, capital‑intensive infrastructure projects driven by energy, data, AI and digital transformation. We will continue to invest in this space as part of our ambition to build a destination practice for the most capital-intensive infrastructure and energy work." Dajani specializes in clean energy, renewable energy and energy transitions. The Loyola University Chicago School of Law graduate's clients include sponsors, utilities, financial institutions, underwriters, private equity funds, infrastructure funds, investment banks, clean technology companies and sovereign wealth funds, and her practice spans project finance and development, mergers and acquisitions, finance and tax equity, according to Cooley. "We're seeing that demand scale in ways we haven't before, particularly alongside digital infrastructure growth, and our focus is on building the platform to meet what our clients will need next — expanding our capabilities to support them as this evolves," Dajani said. --Additional reporting by Christine DeRosa, Emma Cueto and Tracey Read. Editing by Daniel King.

NEW YORK (Capital Markets in Africa): At the BRVM Investment Days held in New York City on 21 April 2026, Capital Markets in Africa sat down for an exclusive conversation with Ms Nafissatou Fall Diagne, Managing Director, Development Finance Advisory. In a...

NEW YORK (Capital Markets in Africa): At the BRVM Investment Days held in New York City on 21 April 2026, Capital Markets in Africa sat down for an exclusive conversation with Ms Nafissatou Fall Diagne, Managing Director, Development Finance Advisory. In a wide‑ranging discussion, she shared her insights on how prepared African issuers are to meet the ESG disclosure and reporting standards demanded by global investors, and explored the emerging opportunities for green, social, and sustainability‑linked bonds across the region. Her perspective reflects the growing sophistication of Africa’s capital markets as they adapt to global sustainability expectations while building credible frameworks for climate‑aligned investment. How prepared are African issuers to meet the ESG disclosure and reporting standards demanded by global investors? NAFISSATOU FALL DIAGNE: African issuers are making progress, but preparedness remains uneven across countries. In some markets, there is increasing alignment with international frameworks promoted by the International Sustainability Standards Board and the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures, reflecting a broader shift toward integrating ESG into financial decision-making. At the same time, the regional regulatory architecture is catching up at the same pace. Côte d’Ivoire has adopted a transition taxonomy, the AMF-UMOA has issued a green, social, and sustainable taxonomy framework for the WAEMU region, and Senegal is in the process of adopting its own green taxonomy. These are not cosmetic moves; they give issuers a stable reference point and signal to investors that the supervisory infrastructure is maturing. While challenges remain, particularly around data, capacity, and implementation, African markets are clearly in a transition phase. For investors, what matters most today is not perfect alignment but transparency, credible transition pathways, and a demonstrated commitment to improving ESG disclosure over time. What are the most promising opportunities for green, social, and sustainability-linked bonds in the region? NAFISSATOU FALL DIAGNE: The opportunity is genuinely large, and it maps directly onto Africa’s investment gap. Both the IEA and the Climate Policy Initiative put the financing requirement for energy, infrastructure, and resilience well into the hundreds of billions annually by 2030, a gap that public finance alone cannot close, which is precisely where capital markets come in. In terms of sectors, the most natural fits are renewable energy, sustainable agriculture, water and sanitation, urban infrastructure, and waste management, areas where there is both a clear environmental or social outcome and a viable revenue model to service the debt. Sustainability-linked bonds have a lot of untapped potential here, because they do not require issuers to ring-fence proceeds to specific projects. They link the cost of debt to performance against pre-agreed KPIs, which suits corporates whose transition cuts across multiple parts of their operations rather than a single asset. Regional platforms like the BRVM have a real role to play in standardization and visibility: building local benchmarks, normalizing second-party opinion processes, and giving international investors a reliable point of entry. But the binding constraint is not appetite but rather the pipeline. Scaling these instruments depends on the steady origination of bankable, ESG-aligned projects, and that is where governments, sponsors, and arrangers need to concentrate their efforts. How can African governments and corporates avoid “greenwashing” while still attracting climate-aligned capital? NAFISSATOU FALL DIAGNE: Credibility is really what determines whether climate-aligned capital flows, and at what scale. The starting point is alignment with the established frameworks, ICMA’s Green, Social and Sustainability-Linked Bond Principles in particular, because they set out what investors expect on use of proceeds, KPI selection, and ongoing disclosure. Adopting them is less about ticking a compliance box than about speaking a language the market understands. From there, the work is practical: defining eligibility criteria narrowly enough so that they mean something, securing a credible second-party opinion before issuance, and reporting on impact annually with the same rigor as financial reporting. Where a sovereign or corporate goes further, anchoring issuance to NDC commitments, publishing its methodologies, and allowing independent verification of outcomes, the pricing benefit tends to follow. The market is increasingly moving beyond labels toward credibility. Investors are looking for integrity, consistency, and demonstrable impact. For African issuers, this is more opportunity than constraint: the continent’s adaptation needs and just-transition narratives are credible and structurally underfunded, and the issuers who can demonstrate measurable outcomes will build the kind of track record that brings repeat investors back. Avoiding greenwashing, in that sense, is not a defensive posture; it is the foundation of a durable funding relationship. The post first appeared on .

urn:contentItem:6JHD-M503-S6WK-11X0-00000-00How Exelon Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisHow Exelon Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

During President Trump's second term, corporate investment in the United States has increased, with major companies in technology, manufacturing, and healthcare allocating billions to domestic expansion. The semiconductor industry has committed over $500 billion in private-sector funding, indicating a notable reshoring trend. This investment activity is backed by enhanced tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, including 100% bonus depreciation and 35% manufacturing credits, and tariff policies aimed at supporting domestic production.

The reshoring trend affects multiple high-value sectors. Semiconductor manufacturing investments of over $500 billion across U.S. facilities are expected to create more than 150,000 new jobs, while pharmaceutical companies have allocated over $150 billion for U.S. manufacturing investments. Foreign direct investment was recorded at $102 billion in Q2 2025, representing a 137% increase from Q1. This comprehensive restructuring is influenced by both policy incentives and supply chain resilience priorities.

This report examines investment commitments from companies across the technology, manufacturing, healthcare, and energy sectors.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ: EXC)

- Company Profile: Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ: EXC) is a U.S.-based utility company that operates electric and gas utilities, with a global workforce of approximately 33,000.

- Announced Investment: $4.5 billion for 2025-2026 to modernize electric grid infrastructure, improve reliability, and increase clean energy integration in its service territories.

- Recipient: Exelon U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ: BKR)

- Company Profile: Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ: BKR) is a U.S.-based energy technology firm that supplies oilfield services and equipment, with about 55,000 employees globally.

- Announced Investment: $1.5 billion is allocated for 2025-2026 to advance carbon capture and hydrogen technology, and expand U.S. manufacturing for energy transition equipment.

- Recipient: Baker Hughes U.S. Energy Transition Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Energy Technology, Manufacturing

Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE: LNG)

- Company Profile: Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE: LNG) is a U.S.-based LNG firm that runs liquefaction and export facilities, employing approximately 1,800 people globally.

- Announced Investment: A $4 billion investment for 2025-2026 is aimed at expanding the Sabine Pass and Corpus Christi LNG facilities to increase export capacity.

- Recipient: Cheniere Energy U.S. LNG Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Energy, LNG Export

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED)

- Company Profile: Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED) is a U.S.-based utility that provides electric, gas, and steam services, with a global employee count of approximately 15,000.

- Announced Investment: $2.8 billion planned for 2025-2026 to upgrade the New York City electric grid, improve resilience to weather, and increase renewable energy connections.

- Recipient: Consolidated Edison U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

Crown Castle International (NYSE: CCI)

- Company Profile: Crown Castle International (NYSE: CCI) is a U.S.-based telecommunications infrastructure firm that manages towers and fiber networks, with a team of approximately 7,000.

- Announced Investment: $3.5 billion for 2025-2026 to build out fiber-to-the-premises networks and 5G infrastructure throughout the U.S.

- Recipient: Crown Castle U.S. Telecom Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Telecommunications, Infrastructure.

Deere and Company

- Company Profile: Deere and Company (NYSE: DE) is a firm specializing in agricultural machinery and equipment manufacturing, employing approximately 82,000 people worldwide.

- Announced Investment: $2 billion in 2025 to enlarge Illinois and Iowa manufacturing facilities for the production of advanced machinery.

- Recipient: Deere U.S. Manufacturing Plants.

- Industries Impacted: Manufacturing, Agriculture.

Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: FANG)

- Company Profile: Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: FANG) is a U.S.-based oil and gas firm concentrated on Permian Basin operations, employing approximately 900 people globally.

- Announced Investment: $3.8 billion in a 2025 capital program for drilling and completion activities in the Permian Basin, increasing production capacity.

- Recipient: Diamondback Energy U.S. Permian Operations.

- Industries Impacted: Energy, Oil and Gas.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE: DTE)

- Company Profile: DTE Energy Company (NYSE: DTE) is a U.S.-based utility firm that supplies electric and gas services, employing approximately 10,000 people worldwide.

- Announced Investment: $2.3 billion for 2025-2026 to increase renewable energy capacity, update the Michigan electric grid, and develop natural gas infrastructure.

- Recipient: DTE Energy U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

FirstEnergy Corp. (NYSE: FE)

- Company Profile: FirstEnergy Corp. is an energy infrastructure operator with a workforce of approximately 15,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A multi-year investment of $15 billion is designated for infrastructure upgrades and grid modernization projects.

- Recipient: FirstEnergy U.S. Energy Grid

- Industries Impacted: Energy

First Solar Inc. (NASDAQ: FSLR)

- Company Profile: First Solar Inc., a U.S.-headquartered manufacturer of solar panels, maintains production facilities worldwide and employs about 6,400 people.

- Announced Investment: An allocation of $1.1 billion in 2025 will fund the construction of a new solar panel manufacturing plant in Ohio. A separate $1.1 billion, 3.5 GW facility in Louisiana is scheduled to be operational in late 2025.

- Recipient: First Solar U.S. Solar Panel Plants

- Industries Impacted: Renewable Energy, Manufacturing

Occidental Petroleum (NYSE: OXY)

- Company Profile: A U.S. energy company with a workforce of approximately 12,000 employees.

- Announced Investment: A $1.8 billion investment is planned for July 2025 for Texas enhanced oil recovery and direct air capture (DAC) plants.

- Recipient: U.S. energy sites

- Industries Impacted: Energy

ONEOK Inc. (NYSE: OKE)

- Company Profile: ONEOK, Inc., a U.S.-based energy company, manages extensive natural gas pipeline networks and employs approximately 3,000 individuals.

- Announced Investment: In August 2025, ONEOK confirmed its involvement in the Eiger Express Pipeline, a $1.2 billion, An error occurred during generation.

- Industries Impacted: Energy

Announced Investment: In August 2025, ONEOK confirmed its involvement in the Eiger Express Pipeline, a $1.2 billion, An error occurred during generation.Industries Impacted: Energy

Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE: PEG)

- Company Profile: Public Service Enterprise Group is a U.S.-based energy firm managing utilities and power generation, employing approximately 13,000 people globally.

- Announced Investment: A sum of $3.2 billion is designated for 2025-2026 to build offshore wind infrastructure, update transmission systems, and increase clean energy generation in New Jersey.

- Recipient: Public Service Enterprise Group U.S. Energy Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Renewable Energy

Rollins Inc. (NYSE: ROL)

- Company Profile: Rollins Inc. (NYSE: ROL) is a U.S.-headquartered pest control and termite services firm, with a global workforce of around 18,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A $1 billion capital injection in 2025 is allocated for fleet modernization and facility expansions.

- Recipient: Rollins U.S. Operations Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Services, Facilities

Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT)

- Company Profile: Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT), a prominent U.S.-headquartered retailer, manages stores and distribution networks with a workforce of around 440,000 employees.

- Announced Investment: A $1 billion development plan for 2025 is intended to improve distribution centers in Minnesota to accommodate e-commerce expansion.

- Recipient: Target U.S. Retail Operations

- Industries Impacted: Retail, Logistics

Teradyne Inc. (NASDAQ: TER)

- Company Profile: Teradyne Inc. (NASDAQ: TER) is a U.S.-headquartered semiconductor test equipment producer, with a global workforce of around 5,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A $1.4 billion capital injection in 2025 is set for the expansion of manufacturing and R&D facilities.

- Recipient: Teradyne U.S. Manufacturing Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Semiconductors, Manufacturing

Xcel Energy Inc. (NASDAQ: XEL)

- Company Profile: Xcel Energy Inc. is a U.S.-based utility company that delivers electricity and natural gas and has a global workforce of approximately 11,000.

- Announced Investment: A $2.5 billion expenditure in 2025-2026 will support renewable energy generation, transmission upgrades, and distribution modernization across its service area.

- Recipient: Xcel Energy U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Renewable Energy.

Zoom Video Communications Inc. (NASDAQ: ZM)

- Company Profile: Zoom Video Communications Inc. is a U.S.-based video conferencing and communications platform company with a global staff of approximately 9,000.

- Announced Investment: A $1.5 billion fund in 2025 is planned for the expansion of data center infrastructure and AI capabilities.

- Recipient: Zoom U.S. Technology Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Technology, Cloud Computing.

A year ago the Exelon Corporation share price was $44.42. $1,000 would have bought 22.5 Exelon Corporation shares on that day. Those 22.5 Shares would be worth $1,040 at today's share price of $46.18. Dividends reinvested are worth $10. PV$1000= $1,049.

Activities

Exelon Corporation is a prominent U.S.-based energy company listed on the Nasdaq, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas delivery. As one of the largest utilities in the nation, it operates through subsidiaries like Commonwealth Edison, PECO, and BGE, serving millions of customers across multiple states. The company's core activities encompass a diverse energy portfolio, including nuclear, fossil fuel, and renewable sources, with a strong emphasis on reliable and sustainable power supply. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Exelon is also advancing grid modernization and smart technology investments to enhance efficiency and integrate more renewables, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon future while maintaining operational excellence and financial stability. It is the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Exelon Corporation, NASDAQ:EXC>

Exelon Corporation's capex plan is strategically crafted, encompassing the EXC capex guidance and a forward-looking Exelon capital expenditure plan. The Exelon 10-year capex plan is an integral part of its capital deployment strategy, emphasizing Exelon rate base growth and infrastructure investment. A focus on Exelon grid modernization projects and smart grid upgrades marks a significant stride in enhancing energy delivery. Key projects include Exelon transmission projects and distribution upgrades, underlining a strong commitment to regulated utility capex. The Exelon nuclear fleet investment along with renewable energy investments affirms the company’s dedication to a sustainable future. Notably, Exelon's battery storage projects further reinforce this commitment, supported by clean energy transition capex. Maintenance and growth capex are pivotal as the capex targets evolve. The company’s capex strategy is outlined in the Exelon investor presentation, impacting both earnings and dividend outlook. As the Exelon stock EXC garners attention, its target price reflects dynamic shifts like rate case impact and capital allocation priorities. The inclusion of Exelon ESG and decarbonization targets highlights its aligned infrastructure spending forecast. Evidently, the story is shifting for Exelon, with capital spending cresting towards 2026.

urn:contentItem:6JHV-3M23-SD9W-Y3RM-00000-00Cooley launches energy, infrastructure and real estate group with Baker Botts energy co-chair2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisNEWSCooley launches energy, infrastructure and real estate group with Baker Botts energy co-chairVictoria Basham

08 May 2026 Cooley has launched an infrastructure, energy and real estate group with the hire of Baker Botts partner Mona Dajani in New York as co-chair. Dajani served as global co-chair of Baker Botts' energy, infrastructure and hydrogen practice, having joined the firm in 2023 following similar energy leadership...

08 May 2026 Cooley has launched an infrastructure, energy and real estate group with the hire of Baker Botts partner Mona Dajani in New York as co-chair. Dajani served as global co-chair of Baker Botts' energy, infrastructure and hydrogen practice, having ...

08 May 2026 Cooley has launched an infrastructure, energy and real estate group with the hire of Baker Botts partner Mona Dajani in New York as co-chair. Dajani served as global co-chair of Baker Botts' energy, infrastructure and hydrogen practice, having joined the firm in 2023 following similar energy leadership roles at A&O Shearman and Pillsbury. Cooley said Dajani would help advance its strategy to offer a "fully integrated, multichannel infrastructure platform". Her hire follows John Goldman joining earlier this year in New York from Kirkland & Ellis, where he led its corporate real estate team. “Mona adds invaluable expertise to our platform,” said Cooley's CEO, Rachel Proffitt. “The infrastructure market is converging where our clients are focused: on complex, capital‑intensive infrastructure projects driven by energy, data, AI and digital transformation. We'll continue to invest in this space as part of our ambition to build a destination practice for the most capital-intensive infrastructure and energy work.” The new practice spans renewable energy and storage development, data centre and digital infrastructure, conventional power generation and grid interconnection as well as project finance, M&A and the deal structures at the intersection of AI and energy. Dajani will co-chair the practice alongside WashingtonDC-based Michelle Schulman, a longtime Cooley partner who previously led its real estate practice. Dajani, who is dual-qualified in the US and England and Wales, brings 25 years of experience advising on energy and infrastructure projects, with a practice spanning project finance and development, M&A, finance and tax equity. Chambers notes she is "well versed in the project financing of renewable assets such as wind, solar and battery storage", with her past work including representing Dutch pension manager APG in its purchase of a major stake in the $1.2bn Gemini project, one of the largest solar projects in the US. She also advised EV fleet charging specialist TeraWatt in raising more than $1bn in financing for the buildout of electric fleet charging centres across the US. Dajani wrote on LinkedIn that her move to Cooley was "a deliberate decision shaped by where the market is going. Across power, renewables, storage and electrification, the system is being reconfigured in real time. Capital is moving toward scale, resilience and integrated platforms, and that is changing how transactions are structured and how risk is assessed. "The clients leading in this space are working with teams that can see across the full platform. Cooley stood out for exactly that alignment at the intersection of capital, infrastructure and innovation, with a genuinely intensive collaborative approach." Dajani's hire follows a record 2025 for Cooley, with the firm growing revenue 10.3% to $2.4bn and profit per equity partner 18.3% to $4.6m. Revenue per lawyer rose 11.5% to $1.8m. Mona Dajani is the general editor of a forthcoming Law Over Borders guide to energy law. For further details, email: [email protected]. Click here for more details about Global Legal Post's Law Over Borders guides. © 2026 The Global Legal Post. All Rights Reserved

urn:contentItem:6JJN-V8C3-S7J2-F0JV-00000-00CPS Energy partners with Israeli construction company on San Antonio solar farm2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisCONSTRUCTION NEWS, CONSTRUCTION NEWS, CONSTRUCTION NEWS, CONSTRUCTION NEWS, CONSTRUCTION NEWS & MIDDLE EAST NEWSCPS Energy partners with Israeli construction company on San Antonio solar farmKelsey Brown, Staff Writer

CPS Energy is partnering with one of Israel’s biggest construction companies to build an 867-acre solar farm on the South Side.

The solar project, El Patrimonio, is a collaboration between CPS Energy, Israeli company Ashtrom Renewable Energy and San Diego-based Solv Energy. The 150-megawatt solar project will be the city-owned utility’s third-largest solar farm.

Ashtrom Renewable will begin operating the farm next year under a 20-year power purchase agreement with CPS Energy, selling the utility 70% of the energy it generates. The project will generate enough energy to power about 37,000 homes, with the remaining 30% of generation to be sold on the Texas open electricity market.

The parent company of Ashtrom Renewables is Ashtrom Group, one of Israel’s biggest construction and real estate companies.

READ MORE:CPS Energy is ramping up community solar, seeking proposals to spark struggling program

CPS Energy’s second-largest source of solar power, Tierra Bonita, also was built by the Israel-based company. CPSgets 180 megawatts from the solar farm.

The utility's largest solar farm is Peregrine Solar, which produces 300 megawatts. CPS struck a 25-year power purchase agreement with New York-based company Con Edison in 2022, who had the solar farm operational a few years later.

The city-owned utility said El Patrimonio is part of its quest to grow its utility-scale renewable energy generation in South Texas.

“CPS Energy’s participation reflects its ongoing efforts to diversify its energy portfolio, enhance system reliability, and support long-term sustainability goals through strategic partnerships that bring new solar resources online for the region,” it said.

Solar currently accounts for 10% of the utility’s portfolio. It has proposed increasing solar to 28% by 2040 as part of its updated generation plan, which hasn’t yet been approved by the utility’s board. The solar project at 676 Neal Road is part of the utility’s plan to meet that goal.

RELATED:CPS Energy revamps power plan, shifts toward more natural gas as demand surges

In November, CPS Energy said it was seeking proposals for 600 megawatts of solar energy to be acquired through power purchase agreements. That would be enough power to provide enough electricity for 150,000 homes on a hot summer day.

El Patrimonio was a response to an earlier request for proposals, with CPS signing the contract for the project in 2023.

CPS Energy also is working to boost the amount of power available through its community solar program. In February, it issued a request for proposals seeking up to 50 megawatts of solar energy, nearly five times as much power as what’s now available through the program.

CPS currently has around 1,041 megawatts of solar, enough to power more than 260,000 homes, dispersed across 16 different sites.

urn:contentItem:6JK2-DTX3-S1SX-D4FB-00000-00CPS Energy expands solar2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisAPg. A009CPS Energy expands solarKelsey BrownStaff Writer

CPS Energy is partnering with one of Israel's biggest construction companies to build an 867-acre solar farm on the South Side. The solar project, El Patrimonio, is a collaboration between CPS Energy, Israeli company Ashtrom Renewable Energy and San Diego-based Solv Energy. The 150-megawatt solar project, which began construction in 2025, will be the city-owned utility's third-largest solar farm.

CPS Energy is partnering with one of Israel's biggest construction companies to build an 867-acre solar farm on the South Side.

The solar project, El Patrimonio, is a collaboration between CPS Energy, Israeli company Ashtrom Renewable Energy and San Diego-based Solv Energy. The 150-megawatt solar project, which began construction in 2025, will be the city-owned utility's third-largest solar farm.

"CPS Energy is the number one buyer of solar energy in Texas," said Frank Almaraz, the utility's chief operating officer. "The partnership with Ashtrom helps maintain our leadership position."

Ashtrom Renewable will begin operating the farm next year under a 20-year power purchase agreement with CPS Energy, selling the utility 70% of the energy it generates. The project will generate enough energy to power about 37,000 homes, with the remaining 30% of generation to be sold on the Texas open electricity market. The utility estimates it will reduce carbon emissions by about 193 tons annually.

"This is an exciting day for Ashtrom Renewable Energy and a major milestone in our continued commitment to delivering sustainable, reliable energy solutions," said Yitsik Mermelstein, CEO of Ashtrom Renewable.

The parent company of Ashtrom Renewable is Ashtrom Group, one of Israel's biggest construction and real estate companies.

CPS Energy's second-largest source of solar power, Tierra Bonita, also was built by the Israel-based company. CPS gets 180 megawatts from the solar farm.

The utility's largest solar farm is Peregrine Solar, which produces 300 megawatts. CPS struck a 25-year power purchase agreement with New York-based company Con Edison in 2022.

Renewable energy is "a key part of delivering on our diverse generation plan to meet our growing population's needs," Almaraz said.

The city-owned utility said El Patrimonio helps "diversify its energy portfolio, enhance system reliability, and support long-term sustainability goals through strategic partnerships that bring new solar resources online for the region."

Solar currently accounts for 10% of the utility's portfolio. It has proposed increasing solar to 28% by 2040 as part of its updated generation plan, which hasn't yet been approved by the utility's board. The solar project at 676 Neal Road is part of the utility's plan to meet that goal.

In November, CPS Energy said it was seeking proposals for 600 megawatts of solar energy to be acquired through power purchase agreements. That would be enough power to provide enough electricity for 150,000 homes on a hot summer day.

El Patrimonio was a response to an earlier request for proposals, with CPS signing the contract for the project in 2023.

CPS Energy also is working to boost the amount of power available through its community solar program. In February, it issued a request for proposals seeking up to 50 megawatts of solar energy, nearly five times as much power as what's now available through the program.

- CBRE Investment Management (CBRE IM) and Accelerate Infrastructure Opportunities (Accelerate) today announced the closing of a $630 million primary capital raise following strong demand from global investors. This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260507061601/en/ The capital raise includes additional investments from funds managed by CBRE IM and new investments from Mubadala Investment...

- CBRE Investment Management (CBRE IM) and Accelerate Infrastructure Opportunities (Accelerate) today announced the closing of a $630 million primary capital raise following strong demand from global investors. This press release features multimedia. View ...

- CBRE Investment Management (CBRE IM) and Accelerate Infrastructure Opportunities (Accelerate) today announced the closing of a $630 million primary capital raise following strong demand from global investors. This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260507061601/en/ The capital raise includes additional investments from funds managed by CBRE IM and new investments from Mubadala Investment Company, Australian Retirement Trust (ART) and other leading global investors, bringing total equity committed to the platform to $1.26 billion. “This capital reflects deep alignment with long-term institutional partners who understand the durability of infrastructure cash flows,” said Brennan Potts, Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Accelerate. “Our strategy is built on acquiring infrastructure sites that underpin digital connectivity, renewable energy, and mobility, and scaling them through disciplined execution. Reaching $1.26 billion in capital commitments gives us the scale and capital certainty to continue to pursue the most compelling infrastructure opportunities.” CBRE IM and the Accelerate management team launched the infrastructure site acquisition strategy in December 2022 and efficiently built a high-quality, diversified portfolio of over 400 infrastructure sites under digital, renewable and transportation infrastructure assets across 47 states. Robert Shaw, Managing Director at CBRE Investment Management, added: "Leveraging the breadth and capabilities of the CBRE platform and a premier management team, Accelerate quickly established itself as the leading infrastructure site platform. CBRE Investment Management is proud to increase our support of Accelerate and welcome Mubadala, ART and our other partners to the platform. This strategic capital raise will enable Accelerate to continue to scale its diversified portfolio of key infrastructure assets and deliver durable infrastructure cash flows to investors.” Giovanni Oddo, Head of Americas, Infrastructure, Mubadala Investment Company, said : “This partnership with CBRE Investment Management further expands our infrastructure exposure in the United States, a key strategic market for Mubadala. The investment aligns with our focus on key infrastructure at the intersection of digital connectivity and energy systems. As demand accelerates across data, power, and mobility, platforms like Accelerate play an important role in enabling the infrastructure required to support a rapidly evolving global economy. We look forward to working alongside our partners to support the platform’s continued growth and deliver long-term value.” About Accelerate Infrastructure Opportunities Accelerate Infrastructure Opportunities acquires, owns, and manages real estate tied to critical infrastructure sites that generate long-term, sustainable cash flow. Since launching its infrastructure strategy in 2022, Accelerate Infrastructure Opportunities has acquired more than 400 sites across 47 states. With offices in Plano, New York City, and Los Angeles, Accelerate helps developers, operators, and owners unlock value and Land Opportunities ™. Visit www.we-are-accelerate.com or follow us on LinkedIn to learn more. About CBRE Investment Management CBRE Investment Management is a leading global real assets investment management firm with $155.2 billion in assets under management* as of March 31, 2026, operating in 20 countries around the world. Through its investor-operator culture, the firm seeks to deliver sustainable investment solutions across real assets categories, geographies, risk profiles and execution formats so that its clients, people and communities thrive. CBRE Investment Management is an independently operated affiliate of CBRE Group, Inc. (NYSE:CBRE), the world’s largest commercial real estate services and investment firm and a premier provider of critical infrastructure services. The company has more than 155,000 employees serving clients in more than 100 countries. CBRE Investment Management harnesses CBRE’s data and market insights, investment sourcing and other resources for the benefit of its clients. For more information, please visit www.cbreim.com. *Assets under management (AUM) refers to the fair market value of real assets-related investments with respect to which CBRE Investment Management provides, on a global basis, oversight, investment management services and other advice and which generally consist of investments in real assets; equity in funds and joint ventures; securities portfolios; operating companies and real assets-related loans. This AUM is intended principally to reflect the extent of CBRE Investment Management’s presence in the global real assets market, and its calculation of AUM may differ from the calculations of other asset managers and from its calculation of regulatory assets under management for purposes of certain regulatory filings. About Mubadala Mubadala Investment Company is a sovereign investor managing a global portfolio, aimed at generating sustainable financial returns for the Government of Abu Dhabi. Mubadala’s US$385 billion (AED 1,414 billion) portfolio spans six continents with interests in multiple sectors and asset classes. We leverage our deep sectoral expertise and long-standing partnerships to drive sustainable growth and profit, while supporting the continued diversification and global integration of the economy of the United Arab Emirates. For more information about Mubadala Investment Company, please visit: www.mubadala.com

Document.Content: urn:contentItem:6JHV-7W83-SFTP-Y31C-00000-00<published>2026-05-28T16:06:21Z</published><updated>2026-05-28T16:06:21Z</updated><author><name>LexisNexis</name></author><content type="application/xml"><!--Transformation version 1.25--><articleDoc xmlns="" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="http://www.lexisnexis.com/xmlschemas/content/public/articledoc/1/" schemaVersion="1.8" xml:lang="en"><articleDocHead><itemInfo><sourceSectionInfo><positionSection>WDL_PAGES</positionSection><positionSequence>Pg. A8</positionSequence></sourceSectionInfo></itemInfo></articleDocHead><nitf:body xmlns:nitf="http://iptc.org/std/NITF/2006-10-18/"><nitf:body.head><nitf:byline><author><person><nameText>CRAIG FOXcfox@wdt.net</nameText></person></author></nitf:byline></nitf:body.head><nitf:body.content><bodyText><p>WATERTOWN The company that had planned to build a giant solar panel manufacturing plant on a site near the Watertown International Airport is going to pay back every penny from a $1.05 million loan that it had with the Jefferson County Industrial Development Agency.</p><p>Convalt Energy defaulted on the loan with the JCIDA after failing to build the 300,000-square-foot plant on the Route 12F site in the town of Hounsfield. The JCIDA subsequently sued the company in state Supreme Court. </p><p>After months of negotiations, the JCIDA's board on Thursday morning approved the "settlement agreement" that calls for paying off the Convalt loan in its entirety. </p><p>The company has already put an undisclosed amount of money in an escrow account and will make the second payment in November to pay off the rest of the loan, JCIDA board chairman Rob Aliasso said before the vote. Board member Edward Waldroff abstained because he became friends with Convalt President Hari Achuthan. </p><p>The amount of the two payments and how much the company will pay in legal bills were not made public on Thursday as the JCIDA waits for a legal document to be forwarded to the agency, said Marshall Weir, CEO of the Jefferson County Local Development Corp.</p><p>"It's everything we needed to complete the matter, but it was not as quick as would have liked," Aliasso said </p><p>The JCIDA had two attorneys working on the settlement agreement for months. Convalt's attorney Ryan T. Donovan could not be reached for comment. </p><p>Despite the lawsuit against the company and its response in court papers, the settlement ended up being worked out amicably, Weir said. </p><p>In November 2024, the JCIDA and Convalt parted ways after the company failed to fulfill its obligations on a land development agreement to build on the 88-acre site. </p><p>The Hounsfield project was once billed as the largest economic development project in Jefferson County's history. </p><p>Plans were announced in February 2021 for Convalt, then a New York City-based renewable energy company, to invest $650 million in the project that would manufacture solar panels, initially creating hundreds of jobs, but projected to employ thousands over time. A sister company, DigiCollect, would also build a facility in the airport industrial park. </p><p>Achuthan blamed a glut of solar products coming into the U.S. from China and deep cuts in product prices, harming the feasibility of the large project. He has also indicated that it was taking more time to obtain financing for the project. He also blamed the COVID pandemic for delaying its start. </p><p>Convalt still owns a nearly 75,000-square-foot building at 511 Pearl St. where it stored solar panel manufacturing equipment that was trucked from a defunct plant in Oregon and was going to be used in its venture here. </p><p>Three Toyota Sequoia SUVs also were left behind in the Pearl Street property's parking lot. The company had a fleet of the SUVs in Watertown when it had a few dozen employees working in Watertown. </p><p>While the Convalt project never went forward, the 88-acre site is shovel-ready with a large pad to build on and sewer and water already available. Weir said the JCIDA "has a couple of leads" interested in the site, but a deal is not imminent.</p></bodyText></nitf:body.content></nitf:body><metadata><dc:metadata xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/"><dc:identifier identifierScheme="DOC-ID">MISHCL-MAG000247832518591B6417EDE29020260508</dc:identifier><dc:source sourceScheme="royaltyReportingKey">0F28DD1EE91DAE53 MISHCL2026</dc:source><dc:identifier identifierScheme="PGUID">urn:contentItem:6JHV-7W83-SFTP-Y31C-00000-00</dc:identifier><dc:source sourceScheme="productContentSetIdentifier">491905</dc:source><dc:date dateType="last-updated">2026-05-08</dc:date></dc:metadata><wordCount number="533"/><publicationInfo><copyright>Distributed by Newsbank, Inc. value.Title: SMA Solar Technology May Consider Portfolio Refinements Following Advanced Energy Industries New York Procurement Dispute *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-FJP3-S0BP-Y0KG-00000-00SMA Solar Technology May Consider Portfolio Refinements Following Advanced Energy Industries New York Procurement Dispute *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisSMA Solar Technology May Consider Portfolio Refinements Following Advanced Energy Industries New York Procurement Dispute *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know:

This situation emphasizes the current regulatory attention on product performance claims within publicly funded renewable energy initiatives. The legal proceedings highlight the potential liabilities when equipment provided for state-supported projects does not meet predetermined standards, leading to a closer review of supply chain disclosures and warranty procedures throughout the solar equipment industry. The results may affect how manufacturers handle contractual duties associated with government funding, which could increase compliance expenses and modify procurement criteria for large-scale solar installations. Wider industry implications might include increased investor attention to quality assurance protocols and risk reporting in emerging energy infrastructure. This situation demonstrates the intersection of state procurement regulations and private legal actions, which could promote more stringent internal testing and documentation procedures across the sector.

Background of the Case

The Otto Candies legal matter began in 2016 after the 2014 dissolution of Oceanografia S.A. de C.V., a Mexican oil-services contractor. Reports note that Oceanografia submitted thousands of falsified Pemex receivables to Citigroup's subsidiary, Grupo Financiero Banamex, which provided approximately $585 million against those invoices through a vendor-finance program. After Mexican authorities suspended Oceanografia in February 2014, Banamex recorded a $400 million loss. Institutional investors, including hedge funds, insurance companies, and pension plans that purchased short-term Banamex-issued certificates, experienced principal losses estimated at $300 million. Claimants state that Citigroup Inc. and senior executives in New York knowingly participated in or recklessly ignored a multi-year enterprise, allegedly violating civil RICO statutes and the Securities Exchange Act. Although the case experienced multiple dismissals due to statute-of-limitations and extraterritoriality concerns, the 11th Circuit reversed those decisions in September 2024, determining that sufficient domestic activity-including New York approvals, U.S. dollar wire transfers, and regulatory filings-occurred to apply RICO domestically. Citigroup submitted a petition for review in November 2024, which remains pending throughout 2025.

Competitive Impacts on SMA Solar Technology

SMA Solar Technology's string inverter solutions function within utility and commercial segments utilizing established European engineering credentials. Industry reports suggest measured customer behavior during U.S. public bidding processes, with a preference for suppliers possessing minimal litigation histories and robust documentation practices.

Possible Opportunities for SMA Solar Technology

Emphasizing European quality certifications during North American public tenders.

Expanding service and monitoring options to differentiate based on long-term reliability.

Targeting joint development projects with municipalities that require proven international vendors.

Utilizing current relationships to attain preferred status in quality-focused requests for proposals.

Why This Case Is Challenging for SMA Solar Technology

A broader procurement focus on litigation history could necessitate additional narrative responses within bid submissions. The alignment of U.S. warranty terms with developing state expectations might require policy modifications. Elevated sector compliance expenses could influence pricing competitiveness. Analyst commentary may integrate comparative litigation risk into the coverage of the inverter peer group.

Key Challenges for SMA Solar Technology

Adapting bid documentation to address concerns regarding quality representations.

Potential increases in product liability insurance premiums.

Allocating resources for improved customer due-diligence support.

Monitoring legal precedents that could affect future public-contract clauses.

Competitor's Strategic Responses

Standardizing quality disclosure packages for all U.S. public bids.

Accelerating North American certification programs.

Refining after-sales support contracts for government clients.

Benchmarking internal warranty language against peer practices.

Key Measures

SMA Solar Technology implements these responses through a cross-regional task force that coordinates U.S. sales strategy with global engineering standards, providing quarterly progress updates to senior management.

Possible Losses and Unexpected Advantages

Potential Advantages

Market share growth in quality-sensitive public projects, translating into additional revenue estimates of up to $30 million.

Improved perception of engineering reliability among public buyers.

Opportunities for premium pricing regarding monitored service packages.

Stronger positioning during competitive utility-scale evaluations.

Potential Losses

Compliance and documentation expenses estimated at up to $18 million.

Possible margin impacts resulting from extended performance guarantees.

Extended sales cycles during increased procurement reviews.

Incremental costs for revised training and collateral materials.

Final Thoughts

SMA Solar Technology navigates manageable indirect effects resulting from increased quality expectations while retaining the technical foundation to utilize this scrutiny as a differentiating factor. Focused execution regarding transparent documentation and service excellence will assist the company in maintaining its position in North American public-sector solar markets.

Tags: The Power Authority of the State of New York, ex rel. Solar Liberty Energy Systems, Inc. v. Advanced Energy Industries, Inc.

Advanced Energy Industries Inc. share has been a three bagger in the past year. A year ago the Advanced Energy Industries Inc. share price was $109.78. $1,000 would have bought 9.1 Advanced Energy Industries Inc. shares on that day. Those 9.1 Shares would be worth $3,148 at today's share price of $345.63. PV$1000= $3,148.

Activities

Advanced Energy Industries Inc., a Nasdaq-listed company, specializes in designing, manufacturing, and supplying precision power conversion, measurement, and control solutions for high-tech industries. Its core business activities focus on enabling advanced manufacturing processes, including semiconductor fabrication, thin film deposition for solar panels and displays, and power management for data centers and industrial applications. The company leverages its expertise in power electronics to support energy-efficient technologies, serving sectors like telecommunications, life sciences, and automotive. Among its fastest-growing segments are data center infrastructure and renewable energy solutions, fueled by rising demand for AI-driven computing and sustainable power systems. This strategic emphasis on innovation and efficiency positions Advanced Energy as a key player in addressing global challenges in energy consumption and technological advancement. It is the NASDAQ's 3rd largest Semi equipment wafer company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JGS-7GB3-S6FN-P3HV-00000-00Pratap Mane: President and Country Head – India, Colt DCS2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:21ZLexisNexisNEWSPratap Mane: President and Country Head – India, Colt DCSshashwat.singh@indiainfrastructure.com

Pratap Mane, President and Country Head – India, Colt DCS With over four decades of experience building large-scale, mission-critical infrastructure across India, Pratap Mane has held senior leadership roles at global banks including J.P. Morgan, Deutsche Bank and...

Pratap Mane, President and Country Head – India, Colt DCS With over four decades of experience building large-scale, mission-critical infrastructure across India, Pratap Mane has held senior leadership roles at global banks including J.P. Morgan, Deutsche ...

Pratap Mane, President and Country Head – India, Colt DCS With over four decades of experience building large-scale, mission-critical infrastructure across India, Pratap Mane has held senior leadership roles at global banks including J.P. Morgan, Deutsche Bank and Barclays; led infrastructure and real estate functions at Bharti Televentures and Airtel Business; and helped establish the Yondr Group’s India data centre platform. As president and country head of Colt Data Centre Services (Colt DCS), he is now focused on expanding hyperscale and artificial intelligence (AI)-ready capacity aligned with global standards for design, sustainability and operational reliability. Since joining Colt DCS in December 2022, Mane has overseen the launch of Phase 1 of the Mumbai campus in September 2023 and structured a joint venture with RMZ to develop more than 250 MW of AI-ready hyperscale capacity across Mumbai and Chennai. He also serves as chair of the Data Centre Committee for the Western Region at the Confederation of Indian Industry. The structural challenges before him are considerable, spanning extreme power demand from AI workloads and rising rack densities; a shortage of specialised talent across operations, design and engineering; and growing regulatory complexity around data protection and resilience. Yet Mane is clear-eyed about the opportunity. “Explosive AI-led demand is accelerating the need for high-density, AI-ready data centre campuses,” he says. On broader trends, Mane points to the convergence of AI and evolving regulatory frameworks as the central force reshaping the data sector. Agentic AI is driving exponential growth in data volumes and compute-intensive workloads, pushing architectures toward low-latency, distributed environments. Data sovereignty, cyber-resilience and sustainability are simultaneously becoming mission-critical priorities, while advances in analytics and automation continue to reshape specialised roles across the industry. His key strengths include combining strategic thinking with execution discipline, translating market opportunities into scalable infrastructure platforms and maintaining a global outlook while remaining closely attuned to India’s complex local market dynamics. Mane holds a bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Mumbai and a master of corporate real estate certification from CoreNet Global in Atlanta. He is a member of the Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors. Outside work, he enjoys music and travel. His wife is a former teacher and his son works in the renewable energy sector in New York.
